1201	  invertible and the dual is a tensor inverse.}
1202	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The isomorphism classes of \(\otimes\)-invertible
1203	  objects of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
1204	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}) form a commutative monoid under the
1205	  substrate tensor product \(\otimes_X\) in which every element is a unit ---
1206	  equivalently, an abelian group. Multiplication is
1207	  \([M]\cdot[N] := [M \otimes_X N]\), well-defined on isomorphism classes
1208	  because \(\otimes_X\) is a bifunctor
1209	  (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}, which transports
1210	  \(M \cong M'\) to \(M \otimes_X N \cong M' \otimes_X N\) and symmetrically)
1211	  and the tensor product of two \(\otimes\)-invertibles is again
1212	  \(\otimes\)-invertible (closure); the unit is \([\mathcal{O}_X]\); and
1213	  associativity, unitality and commutativity are the coherence isomorphisms
1214	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} (on the invertible objects in play),
1215	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} and \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}, read as
1216	  equalities of iso-classes. The inverse of \([M]\) is the class of the tensor
1217	  inverse \(N\) with \(M \otimes_X N \cong \mathcal{O}_X\) carried existentially
1218	  by \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\,M\) (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), so
1219	  every element is a unit. This abelian group is the Picard group \(\Pic(X)\)
1220	  (Stacks tag 01CR). It is, equivalently, the group of units of the commutative
1221	  monoid of \emph{all} iso-classes; that global monoid is not built here, since
1222	  the associator on arbitrary modules is not constructed and the group law
1223	  consumes only the objectwise coherence isomorphisms on invertible objects.
1224	  This mirrors Mathlib's
1225	  \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R = \mathrm{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R))\)
1226	  and the transported \(\mathtt{instCommGroupPic}\), used here as the design
1227	  template; the scheme-level construction is genuinely absent from Mathlib at
1228	  the pinned commit.
1229	
1230	  The associativity datum this group law consumes is the locally-trivial-scoped
1231	  associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}. If the \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\)
1232	  carrier form is retained for the group law, the only added obligation is the
1233	  bridge \(\mathtt{IsInvertible} \Rightarrow \mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) --- the
1234	  standard fact that an invertible sheaf is locally free of rank one --- which
1235	  supplies the local-triviality hypotheses of \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}; this
1236	  bridge is itself off the associator critical path.
1237	\end{lemma}
1238	
1239	\begin{proof}
1240	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
1241	        lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality,
1242	        lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}
1243	  Restrict attention to \(\otimes\)-invertible objects. Closure holds: if
1244	  \(M, N\) are \(\otimes\)-invertible with inverses \(M', N'\), then
1245	  \((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X (M' \otimes_X N') \cong \mathcal{O}_X\) by the
1246	  coherence isomorphisms, so \(M \otimes_X N\) is \(\otimes\)-invertible.
1247	  Well-definedness of \([M]\cdot[N] := [M \otimes_X N]\) on iso-classes is the
1248	  bifunctoriality of \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}. The
1249	  group axioms are propositions about iso-classes and are discharged by the
1250	  coherence isomorphisms, each evaluated on the invertible objects in play:
1251	  associativity by \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} (whose hypotheses are exactly
1252	  \(\otimes\)-invertibility of the three arguments), the unit laws by
1253	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}, and commutativity by
1254	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}; no pentagon, triangle or hexagon identity is
1255	  invoked, since each axiom asserts only the \emph{existence} of an isomorphism.
1256	  Every class \([M]\) has an inverse \([N]\) with
1257	  \([M \otimes_X N] = [\mathcal{O}_X]\), namely the tensor inverse carried by
1258	  \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}(M)\) (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}).
1259	  Therefore the iso-classes of \(\otimes\)-invertible modules form an abelian
1260	  group --- the Picard group of \(X\) (Stacks tag 01CR) --- equivalently the
1261	  group of units of the (un-built) commutative monoid of all iso-classes. The
1262	  whole assembly follows the Mathlib \(\mathtt{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\cdots))\)
1263	  template.
1264	\end{proof}

1266	\section{Consumer: the \(\AddCommGroup\) instance for the relative Picard sheaf}
1267	\label{sec:tensorobj_consumer_acg}
1268	
1269	The substrate of \cref{sec:tensorobj_substrate,sec:tensorobj_onproduct_lift}
1270	supplies all the data missing from \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}'s Lean
1271	encoding. The consumer instance below packages the abelian-group law
1272	\(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]
1273	 := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{C \times_S T} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\) on the
1274	relative Picard quotient via the substrate.
1275	
1276	\begin{theorem}
1277	
1278	\leanok
1279	[Abelian-group instance on the relative Picard quotient via \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)]
1280	  \label{thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj}
1281	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.addCommGroup_via_tensorObj}
1282	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct,
1283	        lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj,
1284	        def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
1285	        lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,
1286	        lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,
1287	        lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,
1288	        lem:tensorobj_comm_iso,
1289	        thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined,
1290	        lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
1291	
1292	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
1293	  % Definitions df:aPf + df:Pfs (the absolute and relative Picard functors are
1294	  % abelian-group-valued; the relative one is defined as a quotient of abelian
1295	  % groups)
1296	  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
1297	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1298	  % "\begin{dfn}\label{df:aPf}
1299	  %  The {\it absolute Picard functor} $\Pic_X$ is the functor from  the
1300	  %  category of (locally Noetherian) $S$-schemes $T$ to the category of
1301	  % abelian groups defined by the formula
1302	  % 	$$\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).$$
1303	  % \end{dfn}
1304	  %  [\dots]
1305	  % \begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
1306	  %   The {\it relative Picard functor} $\Pic_{X/S}$ is defined by
1307	  % 	$$\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).$$
1308	  %  Denote its associated sheaves in the Zariski, \'etale, and fppf
1309	  % topologies  by
1310	  % 	$$\Pic_{(X/S)\zar},\ \Pic_{(X/S)\et},\
1311	  % 	\Pic_{(X/S)\fppf}.$$
1312	  %  \end{dfn}"
1313	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2,
1314	  Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs (the target category is the category of abelian
1315	  groups; the relative Picard functor is a quotient of abelian groups).}
1316	  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve over a
1317	  field \(k\), let \(\pi_C : C \to \Spec k\) be the structure morphism,
1318	  and let \(T\) be a \(k\)-scheme with structure morphism
1319	  \(\pi_T : T \to \Spec k\). The set
1320	  \[
1321	    \Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T) \;\;:=\;\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \,/\, \pi_T^*\Pic(T)
1322	  \]
1323	  of \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined} carries a canonical
1324	  abelian-group structure with addition
1325	  \(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]
1326	   := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{C \times_k T} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\),
1327	  neutral element \(\bigl[\,\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\,\bigr]\), and
1328	  inverse
1329	  \(-\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}^{-1}\,\bigr]\),
1330	  where \(\otimes_{C \times_k T}\) and \((-)^{-1}\) are the operations
1331	  supplied by the substrate of \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
1332	  restricted to the \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) carrier
1333	  via \cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}. With this structure the quotient
1334	  map
1335	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T) \twoheadrightarrow \Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) is a
1336	  surjective group homomorphism with kernel exactly \(\pi_T^*\Pic(T)\),
1337	  in agreement with \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}.
1338	\end{theorem}
1339	
1340	\begin{proof}
1341	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct,
1342	        lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj,
1343	        def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
1344	        lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,
1345	        thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined,
1346	        lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
1347	  Write \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) for the set of isomorphism classes of objects
1348	  of \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) (line bundles on
1349	  \(C \times_k T\)), with the operation
1350	  \([L] + [L'] := [L \otimes_{C \times_k T} L']\). This operation is
1351	  well-defined on isomorphism classes: an isomorphism \(L \cong L_0\) tensors
1352	  to an isomorphism \(L \otimes L' \cong L_0 \otimes L'\) by functoriality of
1353	  \(\otimes\) (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}), and
1354	  symmetrically in the second argument. The abelian-group structure on
1355	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is then exactly the \emph{units group of the
1356	  commutative monoid of \(\otimes\)-iso-classes}
1357	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}): multiplication
1358	  \([L]\cdot[L'] = [L \otimes_{C\times_k T} L']\) is well-defined and
1359	  commutative-monoidal by the coherence isomorphisms
1360	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso},
1361	  and an iso-class is a unit precisely when its carrier is
1362	  \(\otimes\)-invertible (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), the inverse
1363	  being carried by the predicate. Each axiom is a \emph{proposition} --- the
1364	  existence of an isomorphism --- so no monoidal-coherence datum (pentagon,
1365	  triangle, hexagon) is invoked; the \(\AddCommGroup\) on
1366	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is the additive form of this units group. This mirrors
1367	  Mathlib's own Picard group: \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R\) carries its
1368	  \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) (\(\mathtt{instCommGroupPic}\),
1369	  \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}) transported from
1370	  \(\mathrm{Units}\bigl(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R)\bigr)\); here
1371	  the same \(\mathrm{Units}(\cdots)\) shape is transported to the scheme-level
1372	  \(\otimes_X\). (The scheme-level Picard group is genuinely absent from
1373	  Mathlib --- \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup} records ``connect to
1374	  invertible sheaves'' as an open task --- so the project supplies it lightly.)
1375	  The invertibility-predicate carrier is the project's \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\)
1376	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), the scheme-level analogue of
1377	  Mathlib's \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\); the geometric predicate
1378	  \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) is retained separately and is connected to
1379	  \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\) only by an off-critical-path equivalence (only the
1380	  geometric \(\mathtt{IsInvertible} \Leftarrow \mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\)-style
1381	  direction is nontrivial, and it is not needed here).
1382	
1383	  By \cref{lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj}, the pullback functor
1384	  \(\pi_T^{*} : \texttt{LineBundle}\,T \to
1385	   \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) carries tensor products to
1386	  tensor products and the unit to the unit, so the induced map on isomorphism
1387	  classes \(\pi_T^{*} : \Pic(T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T)\) is a group
1388	  homomorphism, and its image is a subgroup.
1389	
1390	  By \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined}, the underlying set of
1391	  \(\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) is the set of cosets of this subgroup. The
1392	  quotient of an abelian group by a subgroup is itself an abelian group, via
1393	  the standard \(\mathtt{QuotientAddGroup}\) construction, and the quotient
1394	  map is a surjective group homomorphism whose kernel is exactly the subgroup
1395	  (Stacks tag 01CR for the Picard-group special case; the general
1396	  quotient-of-abelian-groups statement is in any algebra textbook). Putting
1397	  the pieces together gives the \(\AddCommGroup\) instance on
1398	  \(\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) of the statement, and identifies it with the
1399	  structure of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}.
1400	\end{proof}

838	  This is an objectwise isomorphism for the three fixed locally trivial objects,
839	  not a natural associator on a category: no pentagon identity is constructed or
840	  used. The group law on \(\otimes\)-iso-classes
841	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}) consumes only the existence of this
842	  isomorphism, \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\,\cdots \cong \cdots)\), so this is all that is
843	  required. The construction works \emph{entirely at the presheaf/section level on
844	  a trivialising cover}: each absorption isomorphism comes from checking the
845	  local-on-target injectivity of a whiskered sheafification unit after restricting
846	  to a cover on which the whiskering object is the structure sheaf, then inverting
847	  under \(a\) via \cref{lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W}. It uses
848	  \emph{no} \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R)\) and
849	  \emph{no} open-immersion restriction-compatibility isomorphism
850	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}): because the presheaf tensor is sectionwise,
851	  restriction to the cover is plain evaluation at smaller opens, and no
852	  sheafified-tensor pullback-compatibility iso is needed.

855	\begin{lemma}
856	[Left and right unitors for $\otimes_X$]
857	  \label{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
858	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor, AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_right_unitor}
859	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
860	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) an
861	  \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. With
862	  \(\mathcal{O}_X = \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\) the
863	  structure-sheaf unit object, there are natural isomorphisms
864	  \[
865	    \mathcal{O}_X \otimes_X M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; M,
866	    \qquad
867	    M \otimes_X \mathcal{O}_X \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; M .
868	  \]
869	\end{lemma}
870	
871	\begin{proof}
872	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
873	  The presheaf monoidal structure on \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\) supplies
874	  the left and right unitors \(\lambda, \rho\) at the presheaf level. Their
875	  sheafifications \(\mathtt{sheafification.mapIso}(\lambda)\),
876	  \(\mathtt{sheafification.mapIso}(\rho)\), composed with the sheafification
877	  counit identifying the sheafified structure-sheaf presheaf with
878	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\) (the step already used in the structure-sheaf-unit
879	  comparison \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso}\) in the substrate file), give the
880	  two isomorphisms. As with the associator the route is the cheap
881	  \(\mathtt{mapIso}\) pattern and uses no abstract pullback.
882	\end{proof}
883	
884	\begin{lemma}
885	
886	\leanok
887	[Braiding for $\otimes_X$]

1038	[\(\pi_T^*\) is a tensor-functor]
1039	  \label{lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj}
1040	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}

1042	  % tensor-invertibility) by the standard pullback-tensor compatibility of
1043	  % \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules (Stacks \texttt{lemma-tensor-product-pullback} /
1044	  % \texttt{lemma-pullback-invertible}), independently of the open-immersion
1045	  % restriction-compatibility iso.
1046	  With notation as in \cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}, the pullback
1047	  functor
1048	  \[
1049	    \pi_T^{*} \;\;\colon\;\; \texttt{LineBundle}\,T \;\longrightarrow\;
1050	                          \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T
1051	  \]
1052	  of \cref{def:pullback_along_projection} is a (strong) monoidal
1053	  functor: there is a canonical natural isomorphism
1054	  \(\pi_T^*(\mathcal{N} \otimes_T \mathcal{N'})
1055	     \xrightarrow{\sim}
1056	   \pi_T^* \mathcal{N} \otimes_{C \times_S T} \pi_T^* \mathcal{N'}\)
1057	  for \(\mathcal{N}, \mathcal{N'} \in \texttt{LineBundle}\,T\), and a
1058	  canonical isomorphism
1059	  \(\pi_T^* \mathcal{O}_T \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T}\),
1060	  both inherited from the analogous data on the underlying
1061	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\) functors via the
1062	  pullback-tensor-compatibility of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules.
1063	\end{lemma}
1064	
1065	\begin{proof}
1066	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}
1067	  On any affine open of \(T\) over which \(\mathcal{N}\) and
1068	  \(\mathcal{N'}\) trivialise simultaneously, the assertion reduces to
1069	  the standard fact that the extension of scalars
1070	  \(B \otimes_A (M \otimes_A M')
1071	   \;\cong\; (B \otimes_A M) \otimes_B (B \otimes_A M')\)
1072	  for a ring map \(A \to B\) and \(A\)-modules \(M, M'\). This affine
1073	  identity glues across an affine trivialising cover of \(T\): on each
1074	  affine open where \(\mathcal{N}\) and \(\mathcal{N}'\) are free of rank one
1075	  over \(A\), both sides evaluate to the free rank-one \(B\)-module, so
1076	  the comparison is an isomorphism. No flatness of \(A \to B\) is required
1077	  at this level, as the modules involved are locally free. The compatibility
1078	  with the unit, the associator, and the unitors is verified pointwise on the
1079	  same cover.
1080	\end{proof}

1082	\section{\(\otimes\)-invertibility and the commutative-monoid group-law engine}
1083	\label{sec:tensorobj_invertibility}
1084	
1085	The relative Picard group law is assembled from \emph{\(\otimes\)-invertibility}
1086	and the monoidal coherence isomorphisms of
1087	\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso},
1088	following Mathlib's Picard-group idiom (\(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\),
1089	\(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic} = \mathrm{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R))\),
1090	\(\mathtt{instCommGroupPic}\); \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}) as the
1091	design template. The inverse of an invertible object is carried by the predicate
1092	itself, so neither the open-immersion restriction-compatibility isomorphism
1093	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} nor a separate tensor-inverse lemma is on the
1094	critical path.

1163	  \label{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}

1197	  ``Invertible modules''), Definition~01CX and Lemma~\texttt{lemma-constructions-%
1198	  invertible}. The isomorphism classes of invertible \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
1199	  form an abelian group \(\Pic(X)\) under \(\otimes\), with unit
1200	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\) and inverse the dual; the tensor product of invertibles is
1201	  invertible and the dual is a tensor inverse.}
1202	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The isomorphism classes of \(\otimes\)-invertible
1203	  objects of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
1204	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}) form a commutative monoid under the
1205	  substrate tensor product \(\otimes_X\) in which every element is a unit ---
1206	  equivalently, an abelian group. Multiplication is
1207	  \([M]\cdot[N] := [M \otimes_X N]\), well-defined on isomorphism classes
1208	  because \(\otimes_X\) is a bifunctor
1209	  (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}, which transports
1210	  \(M \cong M'\) to \(M \otimes_X N \cong M' \otimes_X N\) and symmetrically)
1211	  and the tensor product of two \(\otimes\)-invertibles is again
1212	  \(\otimes\)-invertible (closure); the unit is \([\mathcal{O}_X]\); and
1213	  associativity, unitality and commutativity are the coherence isomorphisms
1214	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} (on the invertible objects in play),
1215	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} and \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}, read as
1216	  equalities of iso-classes. The inverse of \([M]\) is the class of the tensor
1217	  inverse \(N\) with \(M \otimes_X N \cong \mathcal{O}_X\) carried existentially
1218	  by \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\,M\) (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), so
1219	  every element is a unit. This abelian group is the Picard group \(\Pic(X)\)
1220	  (Stacks tag 01CR). It is, equivalently, the group of units of the commutative
1221	  monoid of \emph{all} iso-classes; that global monoid is not built here, since
1222	  the associator on arbitrary modules is not constructed and the group law
1223	  consumes only the objectwise coherence isomorphisms on invertible objects.
1224	  This mirrors Mathlib's
1225	  \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R = \mathrm{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R))\)
1226	  and the transported \(\mathtt{instCommGroupPic}\), used here as the design
1227	  template; the scheme-level construction is genuinely absent from Mathlib at
1228	  the pinned commit.
1229	
1230	  The associativity datum this group law consumes is the locally-trivial-scoped
1231	  associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}. If the \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\)
1232	  carrier form is retained for the group law, the only added obligation is the
1233	  bridge \(\mathtt{IsInvertible} \Rightarrow \mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) --- the
1234	  standard fact that an invertible sheaf is locally free of rank one --- which
1235	  supplies the local-triviality hypotheses of \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}; this
1236	  bridge is itself off the associator critical path.
1237	\end{lemma}

1239	\begin{proof}
1240	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
1241	        lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality,
1242	        lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}
1243	  Restrict attention to \(\otimes\)-invertible objects. Closure holds: if
1244	  \(M, N\) are \(\otimes\)-invertible with inverses \(M', N'\), then
1245	  \((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X (M' \otimes_X N') \cong \mathcal{O}_X\) by the
1246	  coherence isomorphisms, so \(M \otimes_X N\) is \(\otimes\)-invertible.
1247	  Well-definedness of \([M]\cdot[N] := [M \otimes_X N]\) on iso-classes is the
1248	  bifunctoriality of \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}. The
1249	  group axioms are propositions about iso-classes and are discharged by the
1250	  coherence isomorphisms, each evaluated on the invertible objects in play:
1251	  associativity by \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} (whose hypotheses are exactly
1252	  \(\otimes\)-invertibility of the three arguments), the unit laws by
1253	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}, and commutativity by
1254	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}; no pentagon, triangle or hexagon identity is
1255	  invoked, since each axiom asserts only the \emph{existence} of an isomorphism.
1256	  Every class \([M]\) has an inverse \([N]\) with
1257	  \([M \otimes_X N] = [\mathcal{O}_X]\), namely the tensor inverse carried by
1258	  \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}(M)\) (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}).
1259	  Therefore the iso-classes of \(\otimes\)-invertible modules form an abelian
1260	  group --- the Picard group of \(X\) (Stacks tag 01CR) --- equivalently the
1261	  group of units of the (un-built) commutative monoid of all iso-classes. The
1262	  whole assembly follows the Mathlib \(\mathtt{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\cdots))\)
1263	  template.
1264	\end{proof}

1266	\section{Consumer: the \(\AddCommGroup\) instance for the relative Picard sheaf}
1267	\label{sec:tensorobj_consumer_acg}
1268	
1269	The substrate of \cref{sec:tensorobj_substrate,sec:tensorobj_onproduct_lift}
1270	supplies all the data missing from \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}'s Lean
1271	encoding. The consumer instance below packages the abelian-group law
1272	\(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]
1273	 := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{C \times_S T} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\) on the
1274	relative Picard quotient via the substrate.
1275	
1276	\begin{theorem}
1277	
1278	\leanok
1279	[Abelian-group instance on the relative Picard quotient via \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)]
1280	  \label{thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj}
1281	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.addCommGroup_via_tensorObj}
1282	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct,
1283	        lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj,
1284	        def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
1285	        lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,
1286	        lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,
1287	        lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,
1288	        lem:tensorobj_comm_iso,
1289	        thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined,
1290	        lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
1291	
1292	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
1293	  % Definitions df:aPf + df:Pfs (the absolute and relative Picard functors are
1294	  % abelian-group-valued; the relative one is defined as a quotient of abelian
1295	  % groups)
1296	  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
1297	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1298	  % "\begin{dfn}\label{df:aPf}
1299	  %  The {\it absolute Picard functor} $\Pic_X$ is the functor from  the
1300	  %  category of (locally Noetherian) $S$-schemes $T$ to the category of
1301	  % abelian groups defined by the formula
1302	  % 	$$\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).$$
1303	  % \end{dfn}
1304	  %  [\dots]
1305	  % \begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
1306	  %   The {\it relative Picard functor} $\Pic_{X/S}$ is defined by
1307	  % 	$$\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).$$
1308	  %  Denote its associated sheaves in the Zariski, \'etale, and fppf
1309	  % topologies  by
1310	  % 	$$\Pic_{(X/S)\zar},\ \Pic_{(X/S)\et},\
1311	  % 	\Pic_{(X/S)\fppf}.$$
1312	  %  \end{dfn}"
1313	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2,
1314	  Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs (the target category is the category of abelian
1315	  groups; the relative Picard functor is a quotient of abelian groups).}
1316	  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve over a
1317	  field \(k\), let \(\pi_C : C \to \Spec k\) be the structure morphism,
1318	  and let \(T\) be a \(k\)-scheme with structure morphism
1319	  \(\pi_T : T \to \Spec k\). The set
1320	  \[
1321	    \Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T) \;\;:=\;\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \,/\, \pi_T^*\Pic(T)
1322	  \]
1323	  of \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined} carries a canonical
1324	  abelian-group structure with addition
1325	  \(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]
1326	   := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{C \times_k T} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\),
1327	  neutral element \(\bigl[\,\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\,\bigr]\), and
1328	  inverse
1329	  \(-\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}^{-1}\,\bigr]\),
1330	  where \(\otimes_{C \times_k T}\) and \((-)^{-1}\) are the operations
1331	  supplied by the substrate of \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
1332	  restricted to the \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) carrier
1333	  via \cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}. With this structure the quotient
1334	  map
1335	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T) \twoheadrightarrow \Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) is a
1336	  surjective group homomorphism with kernel exactly \(\pi_T^*\Pic(T)\),
1337	  in agreement with \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}.
1338	\end{theorem}
1339	
1340	\begin{proof}
1341	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct,
1342	        lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj,
1343	        def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
1344	        lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,
1345	        thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined,
1346	        lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
1347	  Write \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) for the set of isomorphism classes of objects
1348	  of \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) (line bundles on
1349	  \(C \times_k T\)), with the operation
1350	  \([L] + [L'] := [L \otimes_{C \times_k T} L']\). This operation is
1351	  well-defined on isomorphism classes: an isomorphism \(L \cong L_0\) tensors
1352	  to an isomorphism \(L \otimes L' \cong L_0 \otimes L'\) by functoriality of
1353	  \(\otimes\) (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}), and
1354	  symmetrically in the second argument. The abelian-group structure on
1355	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is then exactly the \emph{units group of the
1356	  commutative monoid of \(\otimes\)-iso-classes}
1357	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}): multiplication
1358	  \([L]\cdot[L'] = [L \otimes_{C\times_k T} L']\) is well-defined and
1359	  commutative-monoidal by the coherence isomorphisms
1360	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso},
1361	  and an iso-class is a unit precisely when its carrier is
1362	  \(\otimes\)-invertible (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), the inverse
1363	  being carried by the predicate. Each axiom is a \emph{proposition} --- the
1364	  existence of an isomorphism --- so no monoidal-coherence datum (pentagon,
1365	  triangle, hexagon) is invoked; the \(\AddCommGroup\) on
1366	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is the additive form of this units group. This mirrors
1367	  Mathlib's own Picard group: \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R\) carries its
1368	  \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) (\(\mathtt{instCommGroupPic}\),
1369	  \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}) transported from
1370	  \(\mathrm{Units}\bigl(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R)\bigr)\); here
1371	  the same \(\mathrm{Units}(\cdots)\) shape is transported to the scheme-level
1372	  \(\otimes_X\). (The scheme-level Picard group is genuinely absent from
1373	  Mathlib --- \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup} records ``connect to
1374	  invertible sheaves'' as an open task --- so the project supplies it lightly.)
1375	  The invertibility-predicate carrier is the project's \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\)
1376	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), the scheme-level analogue of
1377	  Mathlib's \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\); the geometric predicate
1378	  \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) is retained separately and is connected to
1379	  \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\) only by an off-critical-path equivalence (only the
1380	  geometric \(\mathtt{IsInvertible} \Leftarrow \mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\)-style
1381	  direction is nontrivial, and it is not needed here).
1382	
1383	  By \cref{lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj}, the pullback functor
1384	  \(\pi_T^{*} : \texttt{LineBundle}\,T \to
1385	   \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) carries tensor products to
1386	  tensor products and the unit to the unit, so the induced map on isomorphism
1387	  classes \(\pi_T^{*} : \Pic(T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T)\) is a group
1388	  homomorphism, and its image is a subgroup.
1389	
1390	  By \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined}, the underlying set of
1391	  \(\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) is the set of cosets of this subgroup. The
1392	  quotient of an abelian group by a subgroup is itself an abelian group, via
1393	  the standard \(\mathtt{QuotientAddGroup}\) construction, and the quotient
1394	  map is a surjective group homomorphism whose kernel is exactly the subgroup
1395	  (Stacks tag 01CR for the Picard-group special case; the general
1396	  quotient-of-abelian-groups statement is in any algebra textbook). Putting
1397	  the pieces together gives the \(\AddCommGroup\) instance on
1398	  \(\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) of the statement, and identifies it with the
1399	  structure of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}.
1400	\end{proof}

839	  not a natural associator on a category: no pentagon identity is constructed or
840	  used. The group law on \(\otimes\)-iso-classes
841	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}) consumes only the existence of this
842	  isomorphism, \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\,\cdots \cong \cdots)\), so this is all that is
843	  required. The construction works \emph{entirely at the presheaf/section level on
844	  a trivialising cover}: each absorption isomorphism comes from checking the
845	  local-on-target injectivity of a whiskered sheafification unit after restricting
846	  to a cover on which the whiskering object is the structure sheaf, then inverting
847	  under \(a\) via \cref{lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W}. It uses
848	  \emph{no} \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R)\) and
849	  \emph{no} open-immersion restriction-compatibility isomorphism
850	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}): because the presheaf tensor is sectionwise,
851	  restriction to the cover is plain evaluation at smaller opens, and no
852	  sheafified-tensor pullback-compatibility iso is needed.

855	\begin{lemma}
856	[Left and right unitors for $\otimes_X$]
857	  \label{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
858	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor, AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_right_unitor}
859	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
860	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) an
861	  \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. With
862	  \(\mathcal{O}_X = \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\) the
863	  structure-sheaf unit object, there are natural isomorphisms
864	  \[
865	    \mathcal{O}_X \otimes_X M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; M,
866	    \qquad
867	    M \otimes_X \mathcal{O}_X \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; M .
868	  \]
869	\end{lemma}
870	
871	\begin{proof}
872	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
873	  The presheaf monoidal structure on \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\) supplies
874	  the left and right unitors \(\lambda, \rho\) at the presheaf level. Their
875	  sheafifications \(\mathtt{sheafification.mapIso}(\lambda)\),
876	  \(\mathtt{sheafification.mapIso}(\rho)\), composed with the sheafification
877	  counit identifying the sheafified structure-sheaf presheaf with
878	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\) (the step already used in the structure-sheaf-unit
879	  comparison \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso}\) in the substrate file), give the
880	  two isomorphisms. As with the associator the route is the cheap
881	  \(\mathtt{mapIso}\) pattern and uses no abstract pullback.
882	\end{proof}

907	\begin{lemma}
908	
909	\leanok
910	[Inverse of an invertible module]
911	  \label{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}
912	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse}
913	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial,
914	        lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
915	  % NOTE: off the critical path for the relative Picard group law. The group
916	  % law uses the tensor-invertibility predicate IsInvertible
917	  % (def:scheme_modules_isinvertible), which carries the inverse existentially,
918	  % so no separate ``a tensor inverse exists'' lemma is required. Retained only
919	  % as a supplement on the geometric line-bundle subcategory.
920	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Tag 01CR (Modules on Ringed Spaces, \S Invertible
921	  % modules), Lemma~lemma-constructions-invertible (item~2: the dual of an
922	  % invertible is invertible and the contraction map is an isomorphism) +
923	  % Definition~definition-pic (the Picard group as the abelian group of
924	  % iso-classes of invertibles)
925	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L4200--L4213 and L4350--L4357).
926	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
927	  % "\begin{lemma}
928	  % \label{lemma-constructions-invertible}
929	  % Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space.
930	  % ...
931	  % \item If $\mathcal{L}$ is an invertible $\mathcal{O}_X$-module, then so is
932	  % $\SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_X)$ and the evaluation map
933	  % $\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X}
934	  % \SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_X) \to \mathcal{O}_X$
935	  % is an isomorphism.
936	  % ...
937	  % \begin{definition}
938	  % \label{definition-pic}
939	  % Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space.
940	  % The {\it Picard group} $\Pic(X)$ of $X$ is the
941	  % abelian group whose elements are isomorphism classes of
942	  % invertible $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules, with addition
943	  % corresponding to tensor product.
944	  % \end{definition}"
945	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag~01CR, Lemma~\texttt{lemma-constructions-%
946	  invertible} (item~2, the dual of an invertible is invertible and the contraction
947	  is an isomorphism) and Definition~\texttt{definition-pic} (the Picard group is
948	  the abelian group of iso-classes of invertible \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules under
949	  tensor product).}
950	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let \(L \in \texttt{LineBundle}\,X\) be a line
951	  bundle. The dual
952	  \[
953	    L^{-1} \;\;:=\;\; \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(L, \mathcal{O}_X)
954	  \]
955	  is again a line bundle on \(X\), and there is a canonical natural
956	  isomorphism
957	  \(\varepsilon_L : L \otimes_X L^{-1} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_X\).
958	  Consequently every line bundle has a two-sided tensor inverse under
959	  \(\otimes_X\), and the monoid \(\bigl(\texttt{LineBundle}\,X / \sim, \otimes_X,
960	  \mathcal{O}_X\bigr)\) of isomorphism classes is an abelian group ---
961	  the Picard group \(\Pic(X)\) (Stacks tag 01CR).
962	\end{lemma}
963	
964	\begin{proof}
965	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
966	  Locally trivial of rank one is preserved by
967	  \(\mathcal{H}om(-, \mathcal{O}_X)\): on a common affine trivialising
968	  cover, the dual of a free rank-one module is again free of rank one.
969	  The contraction map
970	  \(L \otimes_X \mathcal{H}om(L, \mathcal{O}_X) \to \mathcal{O}_X\) is a
971	  morphism in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), and on each open of the
972	  trivialising cover it specialises to the contraction
973	  \(A \otimes_A A \to A\) of free rank-one \(A\)-modules, which is an
974	  isomorphism; hence the contraction is a global isomorphism. The
975	  abelian-group structure on \(\Pic(X) := \texttt{LineBundle}\,X / \sim\)
976	  is then standard (Stacks tag 01CR).
977	\end{proof}

979	\begin{lemma}

841	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}) consumes only the existence of this
842	  isomorphism, \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\,\cdots \cong \cdots)\), so this is all that is
843	  required. The construction works \emph{entirely at the presheaf/section level on

850	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}): because the presheaf tensor is sectionwise,
851	  restriction to the cover is plain evaluation at smaller opens, and no

855	\begin{lemma}
856	[Left and right unitors for $\otimes_X$]
857	  \label{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
858	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor, AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_right_unitor}
859	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
860	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) an
861	  \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. With
862	  \(\mathcal{O}_X = \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\) the
863	  structure-sheaf unit object, there are natural isomorphisms
864	  \[
865	    \mathcal{O}_X \otimes_X M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; M,
866	    \qquad
867	    M \otimes_X \mathcal{O}_X \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; M .
868	  \]
869	\end{lemma}
870	
871	\begin{proof}
872	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
873	  The presheaf monoidal structure on \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\) supplies
874	  the left and right unitors \(\lambda, \rho\) at the presheaf level. Their
875	  sheafifications \(\mathtt{sheafification.mapIso}(\lambda)\),
876	  \(\mathtt{sheafification.mapIso}(\rho)\), composed with the sheafification
877	  counit identifying the sheafified structure-sheaf presheaf with
878	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\) (the step already used in the structure-sheaf-unit
879	  comparison \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso}\) in the substrate file), give the
880	  two isomorphisms. As with the associator the route is the cheap
881	  \(\mathtt{mapIso}\) pattern and uses no abstract pullback.
882	\end{proof}

945	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag~01CR, Lemma~\texttt{lemma-constructions-%
946	  invertible} (item~2, the dual of an invertible is invertible and the contraction
947	  is an isomorphism) and Definition~\texttt{definition-pic} (the Picard group is
948	  the abelian group of iso-classes of invertible \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules under
949	  tensor product).}
950	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let \(L \in \texttt{LineBundle}\,X\) be a line
951	  bundle. The dual
952	  \[
953	    L^{-1} \;\;:=\;\; \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(L, \mathcal{O}_X)
954	  \]
955	  is again a line bundle on \(X\), and there is a canonical natural
956	  isomorphism
957	  \(\varepsilon_L : L \otimes_X L^{-1} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_X\).
958	  Consequently every line bundle has a two-sided tensor inverse under
959	  \(\otimes_X\), and the monoid \(\bigl(\texttt{LineBundle}\,X / \sim, \otimes_X,
960	  \mathcal{O}_X\bigr)\) of isomorphism classes is an abelian group ---
961	  the Picard group \(\Pic(X)\) (Stacks tag 01CR).
962	\end{lemma}
963	
964	\begin{proof}
965	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
966	  Locally trivial of rank one is preserved by
967	  \(\mathcal{H}om(-, \mathcal{O}_X)\): on a common affine trivialising
968	  cover, the dual of a free rank-one module is again free of rank one.
969	  The contraction map
970	  \(L \otimes_X \mathcal{H}om(L, \mathcal{O}_X) \to \mathcal{O}_X\) is a
971	  morphism in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), and on each open of the
972	  trivialising cover it specialises to the contraction
973	  \(A \otimes_A A \to A\) of free rank-one \(A\)-modules, which is an
974	  isomorphism; hence the contraction is a global isomorphism. The
975	  abelian-group structure on \(\Pic(X) := \texttt{LineBundle}\,X / \sim\)
976	  is then standard (Stacks tag 01CR).
977	\end{proof}
978	
979	\begin{lemma}
980

983	  \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\)]

985	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjOnProduct}
986	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
987	        lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,
988	        lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}

990	  % takes `LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT`, which is the IsLocallyTrivial subtype

996	  % NOTE: the coherence isos
997	  % \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}
998	  % and the units-of-monoid engine \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup} supply
999	  % the group-law data. \texttt{IsLocallyTrivial} is a separate predicate,
1000	  % connected to \texttt{IsInvertible} only by an off-critical-path equivalence.
1001	  Let \(C \to S\) and \(T \to S\) be \(S\)-schemes. The carrier is the
1002	  \(\otimes\)-invertible subtype
1003	  \[
1004	    \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T
1005	    \;\;=\;\;
1006	    \bigl\{\,M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_S T)
1007	            \,\bigm|\, \mathtt{IsInvertible}\,M \,\bigr\}
1008	  \]
1009	  of \cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}. The bifunctor
1010	  \(\otimes_{C \times_S T}\) of
1011	  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality} restricts to this subtype:
1012	  if \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\,M\) and \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\,M'\) then
1013	  \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}(M \otimes_{C \times_S T} M')\); the unit
1014	  \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T}\) is invertible; and every invertible module
1015	  carries its own tensor inverse. Hence the isomorphism classes of the subtype
1016	  form the units group of \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup} on
1017	  \(C \times_S T\).
1018	\end{lemma}

1020	\begin{proof}
1021	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
1022	        lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,
1023	        lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
1024	  Closure under \(\otimes\) is the units-closed-under-multiplication statement of
1025	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}: if \(M \otimes N \cong \mathcal{O}\)
1026	  and \(M' \otimes N' \cong \mathcal{O}\), then
1027	  \((M \otimes M') \otimes (N \otimes N') \cong \mathcal{O}\) by the coherence
1028	  isomorphisms, so \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}(M \otimes M')\) with witness
1029	  \(N \otimes N'\). The unit \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T}\) is invertible with
1030	  witness itself (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}), and the tensor inverse is
1031	  carried by the predicate (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}). Thus the
1032	  subtype is a submonoid all of whose elements are units --- the abelian group
1033	  of iso-classes that is the units group of
1034	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup} restricted to \(C \times_S T\).
1035	\end{proof}

1037	\begin{lemma}
1038	[\(\pi_T^*\) is a tensor-functor]
1039	  \label{lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj}
1040	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}
1041	  % NOTE: pullback preserves the structure sheaf and tensor products (and hence
1042	  % tensor-invertibility) by the standard pullback-tensor compatibility of
1043	  % \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules (Stacks \texttt{lemma-tensor-product-pullback} /
1044	  % \texttt{lemma-pullback-invertible}), independently of the open-immersion
1045	  % restriction-compatibility iso.
1046	  With notation as in \cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}, the pullback
1047	  functor
1048	  \[
1049	    \pi_T^{*} \;\;\colon\;\; \texttt{LineBundle}\,T \;\longrightarrow\;
1050	                          \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T
1051	  \]
1052	  of \cref{def:pullback_along_projection} is a (strong) monoidal
1053	  functor: there is a canonical natural isomorphism
1054	  \(\pi_T^*(\mathcal{N} \otimes_T \mathcal{N'})
1055	     \xrightarrow{\sim}
1056	   \pi_T^* \mathcal{N} \otimes_{C \times_S T} \pi_T^* \mathcal{N'}\)
1057	  for \(\mathcal{N}, \mathcal{N'} \in \texttt{LineBundle}\,T\), and a
1058	  canonical isomorphism
1059	  \(\pi_T^* \mathcal{O}_T \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T}\),
1060	  both inherited from the analogous data on the underlying
1061	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\) functors via the
1062	  pullback-tensor-compatibility of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules.
1063	\end{lemma}
1064	
1065	\begin{proof}
1066	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}
1067	  On any affine open of \(T\) over which \(\mathcal{N}\) and
1068	  \(\mathcal{N'}\) trivialise simultaneously, the assertion reduces to
1069	  the standard fact that the extension of scalars
1070	  \(B \otimes_A (M \otimes_A M')
1071	   \;\cong\; (B \otimes_A M) \otimes_B (B \otimes_A M')\)
1072	  for a ring map \(A \to B\) and \(A\)-modules \(M, M'\). This affine
1073	  identity glues across an affine trivialising cover of \(T\): on each
1074	  affine open where \(\mathcal{N}\) and \(\mathcal{N}'\) are free of rank one
1075	  over \(A\), both sides evaluate to the free rank-one \(B\)-module, so
1076	  the comparison is an isomorphism. No flatness of \(A \to B\) is required
1077	  at this level, as the modules involved are locally free. The compatibility
1078	  with the unit, the associator, and the unitors is verified pointwise on the
1079	  same cover.
1080	\end{proof}
1081	
1082	\section{\(\otimes\)-invertibility and the commutative-monoid group-law engine}
1083	\label{sec:tensorobj_invertibility}
1084	
1085	The relative Picard group law is assembled from \emph{\(\otimes\)-invertibility}
1086	and the monoidal coherence isomorphisms of
1087	\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso},
1088	following Mathlib's Picard-group idiom (\(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\),
1089	\(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic} = \mathrm{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R))\),
1090	\(\mathtt{instCommGroupPic}\); \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}) as the
1091	design template. The inverse of an invertible object is carried by the predicate
1092	itself, so neither the open-immersion restriction-compatibility isomorphism
1093	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} nor a separate tensor-inverse lemma is on the
1094	critical path.
1095	
1096	\begin{definition}\leanok
1097	[\(\otimes\)-invertibility of an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module]
1098	  \label{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}
1099	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.IsInvertible}
1100	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}

1102	  % modules), Definition 01CS (definition-invertible) + Lemma 0B8K

1132	  % $\mathcal{O}_X$-module and the module $\mathcal{N}$ in (2) is isomorphic to

1134	  % \end{lemma}"
1135	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01CR (Modules on Ringed Spaces,
1136	  ``Invertible modules''), Definition~01CS and Lemma~0B8K. An invertible
1137	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module is one for which \(\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X}
1138	  (-)\) is an equivalence; equivalently there exists \(\mathcal{N}\) with
1139	  \(\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{N} \cong \mathcal{O}_X\), and
1140	  then \(\mathcal{N} \cong \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L},
1141	  \mathcal{O}_X)\).}
1142	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). We say \(M\)
1143	  is \emph{\(\otimes\)-invertible} when it admits a tensor inverse: there is an
1144	  object \(N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) together with an isomorphism
1145	  \[
1146	    M \otimes_X N \;\cong\;
1147	      \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}
1148	      \;=\; \mathcal{O}_X ,
1149	  \]
1150	  i.e.\ \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}(M) :\equiv \exists\, N,\
1151	  \mathrm{Nonempty}\bigl(M \otimes_X N \cong
1152	  \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\bigr)\), with
1153	  \(\otimes_X\) the substrate of \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}. The
1154	  predicate is a \emph{proposition} carrying its inverse witness existentially;
1155	  consequently the dual/inverse needed by the group law is definitional and no
1156	  separate ``a tensor inverse exists'' lemma is consumed downstream. This is the
1157	  scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\), transported
1158	  one categorical level up to \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) with the substrate
1159	  \(\otimes_X\).
1160	\end{definition}
1161	
1162	\begin{lemma}[The commutative monoid of \(\otimes\)-iso-classes and its units]
1163	  \label{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}
1164	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjIsoclassCommMonoid}
1165	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
1166	        lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality,
1167	        lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}
1168	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Tag 01CR (Modules on Ringed Spaces, \S Invertible
1169	  % modules), Definition 01CX (definition-pic) + Lemma lemma-constructions-
1170	  % invertible (tensor of invertibles is invertible; dual is the inverse)
1171	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L4200-L4213 and L4350-L4357).
1172	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1173	  % "\begin{lemma}
1174	  % \label{lemma-constructions-invertible}
1175	  % Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space.
1176	  % \begin{enumerate}
1177	  % \item If $\mathcal{L}$, $\mathcal{N}$ are invertible
1178	  % $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules, then so is
1179	  % $\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{N}$.
1180	  % \item If $\mathcal{L}$ is an invertible $\mathcal{O}_X$-module, then so is
1181	  % $\SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_X)$ and the evaluation map
1182	  % $\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X}
1183	  % \SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_X) \to \mathcal{O}_X$
1184	  % is an isomorphism.
1185	  % \end{enumerate}
1186	  % \end{lemma}
1187	  % [\dots]
1188	  % \begin{definition}
1189	  % \label{definition-pic}
1190	  % Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space.
1191	  % The {\it Picard group} $\Pic(X)$ of $X$ is the
1192	  % abelian group whose elements are isomorphism classes of
1193	  % invertible $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules, with addition
1194	  % corresponding to tensor product.
1195	  % \end{definition}"
1196	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01CR (Modules on Ringed Spaces,
1197	  ``Invertible modules''), Definition~01CX and Lemma~\texttt{lemma-constructions-%
1198	  invertible}. The isomorphism classes of invertible \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
1199	  form an abelian group \(\Pic(X)\) under \(\otimes\), with unit
1200	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\) and inverse the dual; the tensor product of invertibles is
1201	  invertible and the dual is a tensor inverse.}
1202	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The isomorphism classes of \(\otimes\)-invertible
1203	  objects of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
1204	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}) form a commutative monoid under the
1205	  substrate tensor product \(\otimes_X\) in which every element is a unit ---
1206	  equivalently, an abelian group. Multiplication is
1207	  \([M]\cdot[N] := [M \otimes_X N]\), well-defined on isomorphism classes
1208	  because \(\otimes_X\) is a bifunctor
1209	  (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}, which transports
1210	  \(M \cong M'\) to \(M \otimes_X N \cong M' \otimes_X N\) and symmetrically)
1211	  and the tensor product of two \(\otimes\)-invertibles is again
1212	  \(\otimes\)-invertible (closure); the unit is \([\mathcal{O}_X]\); and
1213	  associativity, unitality and commutativity are the coherence isomorphisms
1214	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} (on the invertible objects in play),
1215	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} and \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}, read as
1216	  equalities of iso-classes. The inverse of \([M]\) is the class of the tensor
1217	  inverse \(N\) with \(M \otimes_X N \cong \mathcal{O}_X\) carried existentially
1218	  by \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\,M\) (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), so
1219	  every element is a unit. This abelian group is the Picard group \(\Pic(X)\)
1220	  (Stacks tag 01CR). It is, equivalently, the group of units of the commutative
1221	  monoid of \emph{all} iso-classes; that global monoid is not built here, since
1222	  the associator on arbitrary modules is not constructed and the group law
1223	  consumes only the objectwise coherence isomorphisms on invertible objects.
1224	  This mirrors Mathlib's
1225	  \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R = \mathrm{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R))\)
1226	  and the transported \(\mathtt{instCommGroupPic}\), used here as the design
1227	  template; the scheme-level construction is genuinely absent from Mathlib at
1228	  the pinned commit.
1229	
1230	  The associativity datum this group law consumes is the locally-trivial-scoped
1231	  associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}. If the \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\)
1232	  carrier form is retained for the group law, the only added obligation is the
1233	  bridge \(\mathtt{IsInvertible} \Rightarrow \mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) --- the
1234	  standard fact that an invertible sheaf is locally free of rank one --- which
1235	  supplies the local-triviality hypotheses of \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}; this
1236	  bridge is itself off the associator critical path.
1237	\end{lemma}
1238	
1239	\begin{proof}
1240	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
1241	        lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality,
1242	        lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}
1243	  Restrict attention to \(\otimes\)-invertible objects. Closure holds: if
1244	  \(M, N\) are \(\otimes\)-invertible with inverses \(M', N'\), then
1245	  \((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X (M' \otimes_X N') \cong \mathcal{O}_X\) by the
1246	  coherence isomorphisms, so \(M \otimes_X N\) is \(\otimes\)-invertible.
1247	  Well-definedness of \([M]\cdot[N] := [M \otimes_X N]\) on iso-classes is the
1248	  bifunctoriality of \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}. The
1249	  group axioms are propositions about iso-classes and are discharged by the
1250	  coherence isomorphisms, each evaluated on the invertible objects in play:
1251	  associativity by \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} (whose hypotheses are exactly
1252	  \(\otimes\)-invertibility of the three arguments), the unit laws by
1253	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}, and commutativity by
1254	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}; no pentagon, triangle or hexagon identity is
1255	  invoked, since each axiom asserts only the \emph{existence} of an isomorphism.
1256	  Every class \([M]\) has an inverse \([N]\) with
1257	  \([M \otimes_X N] = [\mathcal{O}_X]\), namely the tensor inverse carried by
1258	  \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}(M)\) (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}).
1259	  Therefore the iso-classes of \(\otimes\)-invertible modules form an abelian
1260	  group --- the Picard group of \(X\) (Stacks tag 01CR) --- equivalently the
1261	  group of units of the (un-built) commutative monoid of all iso-classes. The
1262	  whole assembly follows the Mathlib \(\mathtt{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\cdots))\)
1263	  template.
1264	\end{proof}

1266	\section{Consumer: the \(\AddCommGroup\) instance for the relative Picard sheaf}
1267	\label{sec:tensorobj_consumer_acg}
1268	
1269	The substrate of \cref{sec:tensorobj_substrate,sec:tensorobj_onproduct_lift}
1270	supplies all the data missing from \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}'s Lean
1271	encoding. The consumer instance below packages the abelian-group law
1272	\(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]
1273	 := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{C \times_S T} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\) on the
1274	relative Picard quotient via the substrate.
1275	
1276	\begin{theorem}
1277	
1278	\leanok
1279	[Abelian-group instance on the relative Picard quotient via \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)]
1280	  \label{thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj}
1281	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.addCommGroup_via_tensorObj}
1282	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct,
1283	        lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj,
1284	        def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
1285	        lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,
1286	        lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,
1287	        lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,
1288	        lem:tensorobj_comm_iso,
1289	        thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined,
1290	        lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
1291	
1292	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
1293	  % Definitions df:aPf + df:Pfs (the absolute and relative Picard functors are
1294	  % abelian-group-valued; the relative one is defined as a quotient of abelian
1295	  % groups)
1296	  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
1297	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1298	  % "\begin{dfn}\label{df:aPf}
1299	  %  The {\it absolute Picard functor} $\Pic_X$ is the functor from  the
1300	  %  category of (locally Noetherian) $S$-schemes $T$ to the category of
1301	  % abelian groups defined by the formula
1302	  % 	$$\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).$$
1303	  % \end{dfn}
1304	  %  [\dots]
1305	  % \begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
1306	  %   The {\it relative Picard functor} $\Pic_{X/S}$ is defined by
1307	  % 	$$\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).$$
1308	  %  Denote its associated sheaves in the Zariski, \'etale, and fppf
1309	  % topologies  by
1310	  % 	$$\Pic_{(X/S)\zar},\ \Pic_{(X/S)\et},\
1311	  % 	\Pic_{(X/S)\fppf}.$$
1312	  %  \end{dfn}"
1313	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2,
1314	  Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs (the target category is the category of abelian
1315	  groups; the relative Picard functor is a quotient of abelian groups).}
1316	  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve over a
1317	  field \(k\), let \(\pi_C : C \to \Spec k\) be the structure morphism,
1318	  and let \(T\) be a \(k\)-scheme with structure morphism
1319	  \(\pi_T : T \to \Spec k\). The set
1320	  \[
1321	    \Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T) \;\;:=\;\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \,/\, \pi_T^*\Pic(T)
1322	  \]
1323	  of \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined} carries a canonical
1324	  abelian-group structure with addition
1325	  \(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]
1326	   := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{C \times_k T} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\),
1327	  neutral element \(\bigl[\,\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\,\bigr]\), and
1328	  inverse
1329	  \(-\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}^{-1}\,\bigr]\),
1330	  where \(\otimes_{C \times_k T}\) and \((-)^{-1}\) are the operations
1331	  supplied by the substrate of \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
1332	  restricted to the \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) carrier
1333	  via \cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}. With this structure the quotient
1334	  map
1335	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T) \twoheadrightarrow \Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) is a
1336	  surjective group homomorphism with kernel exactly \(\pi_T^*\Pic(T)\),
1337	  in agreement with \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}.
1338	\end{theorem}
1339	
1340	\begin{proof}
1341	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct,
1342	        lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj,
1343	        def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
1344	        lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,
1345	        thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined,
1346	        lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
1347	  Write \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) for the set of isomorphism classes of objects
1348	  of \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) (line bundles on
1349	  \(C \times_k T\)), with the operation
1350	  \([L] + [L'] := [L \otimes_{C \times_k T} L']\). This operation is
1351	  well-defined on isomorphism classes: an isomorphism \(L \cong L_0\) tensors
1352	  to an isomorphism \(L \otimes L' \cong L_0 \otimes L'\) by functoriality of
1353	  \(\otimes\) (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}), and
1354	  symmetrically in the second argument. The abelian-group structure on
1355	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is then exactly the \emph{units group of the
1356	  commutative monoid of \(\otimes\)-iso-classes}
1357	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}): multiplication
1358	  \([L]\cdot[L'] = [L \otimes_{C\times_k T} L']\) is well-defined and
1359	  commutative-monoidal by the coherence isomorphisms
1360	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso},
1361	  and an iso-class is a unit precisely when its carrier is
1362	  \(\otimes\)-invertible (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), the inverse
1363	  being carried by the predicate. Each axiom is a \emph{proposition} --- the
1364	  existence of an isomorphism --- so no monoidal-coherence datum (pentagon,
1365	  triangle, hexagon) is invoked; the \(\AddCommGroup\) on
1366	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is the additive form of this units group. This mirrors
1367	  Mathlib's own Picard group: \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R\) carries its
1368	  \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) (\(\mathtt{instCommGroupPic}\),
1369	  \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}) transported from
1370	  \(\mathrm{Units}\bigl(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R)\bigr)\); here
1371	  the same \(\mathrm{Units}(\cdots)\) shape is transported to the scheme-level
1372	  \(\otimes_X\). (The scheme-level Picard group is genuinely absent from
1373	  Mathlib --- \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup} records ``connect to
1374	  invertible sheaves'' as an open task --- so the project supplies it lightly.)
1375	  The invertibility-predicate carrier is the project's \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\)
1376	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), the scheme-level analogue of
1377	  Mathlib's \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\); the geometric predicate
1378	  \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) is retained separately and is connected to
1379	  \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\) only by an off-critical-path equivalence (only the
1380	  geometric \(\mathtt{IsInvertible} \Leftarrow \mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\)-style
1381	  direction is nontrivial, and it is not needed here).
1382	
1383	  By \cref{lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj}, the pullback functor
1384	  \(\pi_T^{*} : \texttt{LineBundle}\,T \to
1385	   \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) carries tensor products to
1386	  tensor products and the unit to the unit, so the induced map on isomorphism
1387	  classes \(\pi_T^{*} : \Pic(T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T)\) is a group
1388	  homomorphism, and its image is a subgroup.
1389	
1390	  By \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined}, the underlying set of
1391	  \(\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) is the set of cosets of this subgroup. The
1392	  quotient of an abelian group by a subgroup is itself an abelian group, via
1393	  the standard \(\mathtt{QuotientAddGroup}\) construction, and the quotient
1394	  map is a surjective group homomorphism whose kernel is exactly the subgroup
1395	  (Stacks tag 01CR for the Picard-group special case; the general
1396	  quotient-of-abelian-groups statement is in any algebra textbook). Putting
1397	  the pieces together gives the \(\AddCommGroup\) instance on
1398	  \(\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) of the statement, and identifies it with the
1399	  structure of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}.
1400	\end{proof}

945	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag~01CR, Lemma~\texttt{lemma-constructions-%
946	  invertible} (item~2, the dual of an invertible is invertible and the contraction
947	  is an isomorphism) and Definition~\texttt{definition-pic} (the Picard group is
948	  the abelian group of iso-classes of invertible \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules under
949	  tensor product).}
950	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let \(L \in \texttt{LineBundle}\,X\) be a line
951	  bundle. The dual
952	  \[
953	    L^{-1} \;\;:=\;\; \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(L, \mathcal{O}_X)
954	  \]
955	  is again a line bundle on \(X\), and there is a canonical natural
956	  isomorphism
957	  \(\varepsilon_L : L \otimes_X L^{-1} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_X\).
958	  Consequently every line bundle has a two-sided tensor inverse under
959	  \(\otimes_X\), and the monoid \(\bigl(\texttt{LineBundle}\,X / \sim, \otimes_X,
960	  \mathcal{O}_X\bigr)\) of isomorphism classes is an abelian group ---
961	  the Picard group \(\Pic(X)\) (Stacks tag 01CR).
962	\end{lemma}
963	
964	\begin{proof}
965	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
966	  Locally trivial of rank one is preserved by
967	  \(\mathcal{H}om(-, \mathcal{O}_X)\): on a common affine trivialising
968	  cover, the dual of a free rank-one module is again free of rank one.
969	  The contraction map
970	  \(L \otimes_X \mathcal{H}om(L, \mathcal{O}_X) \to \mathcal{O}_X\) is a
971	  morphism in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), and on each open of the
972	  trivialising cover it specialises to the contraction
973	  \(A \otimes_A A \to A\) of free rank-one \(A\)-modules, which is an
974	  isomorphism; hence the contraction is a global isomorphism. The
975	  abelian-group structure on \(\Pic(X) := \texttt{LineBundle}\,X / \sim\)
976	  is then standard (Stacks tag 01CR).
977	\end{proof}

979	\begin{lemma}
980	
981	\leanok
982	[Closure of \(\otimes\) on the \(\otimes\)-invertible carrier
983	  \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\)]
984	  \label{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}
985	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjOnProduct}
986	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
987	        lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,
988	        lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
989	  % NOTE (iter-213): prose↔Lean predicate mismatch. The Lean `tensorObjOnProduct`
990	  % takes `LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT`, which is the IsLocallyTrivial subtype
991	  % (defined in LineBundlePullback.lean), and its body uses only
992	  % `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` directly — NOT `IsInvertible`,
993	  % `tensorObjIsoclassCommMonoid`, or the coherence isos as the prose/\uses below
994	  % claim. Blueprint-writer should reconcile the carrier predicate (IsLocallyTrivial
995	  % vs IsInvertible) and the \uses. See task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-ts213.md.
996	  % NOTE: the coherence isos
997	  % \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}
998	  % and the units-of-monoid engine \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup} supply
999	  % the group-law data. \texttt{IsLocallyTrivial} is a separate predicate,
1000	  % connected to \texttt{IsInvertible} only by an off-critical-path equivalence.
1001	  Let \(C \to S\) and \(T \to S\) be \(S\)-schemes. The carrier is the
1002	  \(\otimes\)-invertible subtype
1003	  \[
1004	    \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T
1005	    \;\;=\;\;
1006	    \bigl\{\,M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_S T)
1007	            \,\bigm|\, \mathtt{IsInvertible}\,M \,\bigr\}
1008	  \]
1009	  of \cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}. The bifunctor
1010	  \(\otimes_{C \times_S T}\) of
1011	  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality} restricts to this subtype:
1012	  if \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\,M\) and \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\,M'\) then
1013	  \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}(M \otimes_{C \times_S T} M')\); the unit
1014	  \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T}\) is invertible; and every invertible module
1015	  carries its own tensor inverse. Hence the isomorphism classes of the subtype
1016	  form the units group of \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup} on
1017	  \(C \times_S T\).
1018	\end{lemma}

1020	\begin{proof}
1021	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
1022	        lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,
1023	        lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
1024	  Closure under \(\otimes\) is the units-closed-under-multiplication statement of
1025	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}: if \(M \otimes N \cong \mathcal{O}\)
1026	  and \(M' \otimes N' \cong \mathcal{O}\), then
1027	  \((M \otimes M') \otimes (N \otimes N') \cong \mathcal{O}\) by the coherence
1028	  isomorphisms, so \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}(M \otimes M')\) with witness
1029	  \(N \otimes N'\). The unit \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T}\) is invertible with
1030	  witness itself (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}), and the tensor inverse is
1031	  carried by the predicate (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}). Thus the
1032	  subtype is a submonoid all of whose elements are units --- the abelian group
1033	  of iso-classes that is the units group of
1034	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup} restricted to \(C \times_S T\).
1035	\end{proof}

1037	\begin{lemma}
1038	[\(\pi_T^*\) is a tensor-functor]
1039	  \label{lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj}
1040	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}
1041	  % NOTE: pullback preserves the structure sheaf and tensor products (and hence
1042	  % tensor-invertibility) by the standard pullback-tensor compatibility of
1043	  % \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules (Stacks \texttt{lemma-tensor-product-pullback} /
1044	  % \texttt{lemma-pullback-invertible}), independently of the open-immersion
1045	  % restriction-compatibility iso.
1046	  With notation as in \cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}, the pullback
1047	  functor
1048	  \[
1049	    \pi_T^{*} \;\;\colon\;\; \texttt{LineBundle}\,T \;\longrightarrow\;
1050	                          \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T
1051	  \]
1052	  of \cref{def:pullback_along_projection} is a (strong) monoidal
1053	  functor: there is a canonical natural isomorphism
1054	  \(\pi_T^*(\mathcal{N} \otimes_T \mathcal{N'})
1055	     \xrightarrow{\sim}
1056	   \pi_T^* \mathcal{N} \otimes_{C \times_S T} \pi_T^* \mathcal{N'}\)
1057	  for \(\mathcal{N}, \mathcal{N'} \in \texttt{LineBundle}\,T\), and a
1058	  canonical isomorphism
1059	  \(\pi_T^* \mathcal{O}_T \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T}\),
1060	  both inherited from the analogous data on the underlying
1061	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\) functors via the
1062	  pullback-tensor-compatibility of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules.
1063	\end{lemma}
1064	
1065	\begin{proof}
1066	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}
1067	  On any affine open of \(T\) over which \(\mathcal{N}\) and
1068	  \(\mathcal{N'}\) trivialise simultaneously, the assertion reduces to
1069	  the standard fact that the extension of scalars
1070	  \(B \otimes_A (M \otimes_A M')
1071	   \;\cong\; (B \otimes_A M) \otimes_B (B \otimes_A M')\)
1072	  for a ring map \(A \to B\) and \(A\)-modules \(M, M'\). This affine
1073	  identity glues across an affine trivialising cover of \(T\): on each
1074	  affine open where \(\mathcal{N}\) and \(\mathcal{N}'\) are free of rank one
1075	  over \(A\), both sides evaluate to the free rank-one \(B\)-module, so
1076	  the comparison is an isomorphism. No flatness of \(A \to B\) is required
1077	  at this level, as the modules involved are locally free. The compatibility
1078	  with the unit, the associator, and the unitors is verified pointwise on the
1079	  same cover.
1080	\end{proof}

1082	\section{\(\otimes\)-invertibility and the commutative-monoid group-law engine}
1083	\label{sec:tensorobj_invertibility}
1084	
1085	The relative Picard group law is assembled from \emph{\(\otimes\)-invertibility}
1086	and the monoidal coherence isomorphisms of
1087	\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso},
1088	following Mathlib's Picard-group idiom (\(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\),
1089	\(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic} = \mathrm{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R))\),
1090	\(\mathtt{instCommGroupPic}\); \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}) as the
1091	design template. The inverse of an invertible object is carried by the predicate
1092	itself, so neither the open-immersion restriction-compatibility isomorphism
1093	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} nor a separate tensor-inverse lemma is on the
1094	critical path.
1095

1100	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}

1102	  % modules), Definition 01CS (definition-invertible) + Lemma 0B8K
1103	  % (lemma-invertible, the "$\exists\,\mathcal{N}$" characterisation)
1104	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L4046-L4079).
1105	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1106	  % "\begin{definition}

1115	  % $$

1118	  % to $\mathcal{O}_X$.

1120	  % [\dots]
1121	  % \begin{lemma}

1135	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01CR (Modules on Ringed Spaces,
1136	  ``Invertible modules''), Definition~01CS and Lemma~0B8K. An invertible
1137	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module is one for which \(\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X}
1138	  (-)\) is an equivalence; equivalently there exists \(\mathcal{N}\) with
1139	  \(\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{N} \cong \mathcal{O}_X\), and
1140	  then \(\mathcal{N} \cong \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L},
1141	  \mathcal{O}_X)\).}
1142	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). We say \(M\)
1143	  is \emph{\(\otimes\)-invertible} when it admits a tensor inverse: there is an
1144	  object \(N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) together with an isomorphism
1145	  \[
1146	    M \otimes_X N \;\cong\;
1147	      \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}
1148	      \;=\; \mathcal{O}_X ,
1149	  \]
1150	  i.e.\ \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}(M) :\equiv \exists\, N,\
1151	  \mathrm{Nonempty}\bigl(M \otimes_X N \cong
1152	  \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\bigr)\), with
1153	  \(\otimes_X\) the substrate of \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}. The
1154	  predicate is a \emph{proposition} carrying its inverse witness existentially;
1155	  consequently the dual/inverse needed by the group law is definitional and no
1156	  separate ``a tensor inverse exists'' lemma is consumed downstream. This is the
1157	  scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\), transported
1158	  one categorical level up to \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) with the substrate
1159	  \(\otimes_X\).
