1162	\begin{lemma}[The commutative monoid of \(\otimes\)-iso-classes and its units]
1163	  \label{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}
1164	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjIsoclassCommMonoid}
1165	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
1166	        lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality,
1167	        lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}
1168	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Tag 01CR (Modules on Ringed Spaces, \S Invertible
1169	  % modules), Definition 01CX (definition-pic) + Lemma lemma-constructions-
1170	  % invertible (tensor of invertibles is invertible; dual is the inverse)
1171	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L4200-L4213 and L4350-L4357).
1172	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1173	  % "\begin{lemma}
1174	  % \label{lemma-constructions-invertible}
1175	  % Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space.
1176	  % \begin{enumerate}
1177	  % \item If $\mathcal{L}$, $\mathcal{N}$ are invertible
1178	  % $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules, then so is
1179	  % $\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{N}$.
1180	  % \item If $\mathcal{L}$ is an invertible $\mathcal{O}_X$-module, then so is
1181	  % $\SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_X)$ and the evaluation map
1182	  % $\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X}
1183	  % \SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_X) \to \mathcal{O}_X$
1184	  % is an isomorphism.
1185	  % \end{enumerate}
1186	  % \end{lemma}
1187	  % [\dots]
1188	  % \begin{definition}
1189	  % \label{definition-pic}
1190	  % Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space.
1191	  % The {\it Picard group} $\Pic(X)$ of $X$ is the
1192	  % abelian group whose elements are isomorphism classes of
1193	  % invertible $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules, with addition
1194	  % corresponding to tensor product.
1195	  % \end{definition}"
1196	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01CR (Modules on Ringed Spaces,
1197	  ``Invertible modules''), Definition~01CX and Lemma~\texttt{lemma-constructions-%
1198	  invertible}. The isomorphism classes of invertible \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
1199	  form an abelian group \(\Pic(X)\) under \(\otimes\), with unit
1200	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\) and inverse the dual; the tensor product of invertibles is
1201	  invertible and the dual is a tensor inverse.}
1202	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The isomorphism classes of \(\otimes\)-invertible
1203	  objects of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
1204	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}) form a commutative monoid under the
1205	  substrate tensor product \(\otimes_X\) in which every element is a unit ---
1206	  equivalently, an abelian group. Multiplication is
1207	  \([M]\cdot[N] := [M \otimes_X N]\), well-defined on isomorphism classes
1208	  because \(\otimes_X\) is a bifunctor
1209	  (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}, which transports
1210	  \(M \cong M'\) to \(M \otimes_X N \cong M' \otimes_X N\) and symmetrically)
1211	  and the tensor product of two \(\otimes\)-invertibles is again
1212	  \(\otimes\)-invertible (closure); the unit is \([\mathcal{O}_X]\); and
1213	  associativity, unitality and commutativity are the coherence isomorphisms
1214	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} (on the invertible objects in play),
1215	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} and \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}, read as
1216	  equalities of iso-classes. The inverse of \([M]\) is the class of the tensor
1217	  inverse \(N\) with \(M \otimes_X N \cong \mathcal{O}_X\) carried existentially
1218	  by \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\,M\) (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), so
1219	  every element is a unit. This abelian group is the Picard group \(\Pic(X)\)
1220	  (Stacks tag 01CR). It is, equivalently, the group of units of the commutative
1221	  monoid of \emph{all} iso-classes; that global monoid is not built here, since
1222	  the associator on arbitrary modules is not constructed and the group law
1223	  consumes only the objectwise coherence isomorphisms on invertible objects.
1224	  This mirrors Mathlib's
1225	  \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R = \mathrm{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R))\)
1226	  and the transported \(\mathtt{instCommGroupPic}\), used here as the design
1227	  template; the scheme-level construction is genuinely absent from Mathlib at
1228	  the pinned commit.
1229	
1230	  The associativity datum this group law consumes is the locally-trivial-scoped
1231	  associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}. If the \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\)
1232	  carrier form is retained for the group law, the only added obligation is the
1233	  bridge \(\mathtt{IsInvertible} \Rightarrow \mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) --- the
1234	  standard fact that an invertible sheaf is locally free of rank one --- which
1235	  supplies the local-triviality hypotheses of \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}; this
1236	  bridge is itself off the associator critical path.
1237	\end{lemma}
1238	
1239	\begin{proof}
1240	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
1241	        lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality,
1242	        lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}
1243	  Restrict attention to \(\otimes\)-invertible objects. Closure holds: if
1244	  \(M, N\) are \(\otimes\)-invertible with inverses \(M', N'\), then
1245	  \((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X (M' \otimes_X N') \cong \mathcal{O}_X\) by the
1246	  coherence isomorphisms, so \(M \otimes_X N\) is \(\otimes\)-invertible.
1247	  Well-definedness of \([M]\cdot[N] := [M \otimes_X N]\) on iso-classes is the
1248	  bifunctoriality of \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}. The
1249	  group axioms are propositions about iso-classes and are discharged by the
1250	  coherence isomorphisms, each evaluated on the invertible objects in play:
1251	  associativity by \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} (whose hypotheses are exactly
1252	  \(\otimes\)-invertibility of the three arguments), the unit laws by
1253	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}, and commutativity by
1254	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}; no pentagon, triangle or hexagon identity is
1255	  invoked, since each axiom asserts only the \emph{existence} of an isomorphism.
1256	  Every class \([M]\) has an inverse \([N]\) with
1257	  \([M \otimes_X N] = [\mathcal{O}_X]\), namely the tensor inverse carried by
1258	  \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}(M)\) (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}).
1259	  Therefore the iso-classes of \(\otimes\)-invertible modules form an abelian
1260	  group --- the Picard group of \(X\) (Stacks tag 01CR) --- equivalently the
1261	  group of units of the (un-built) commutative monoid of all iso-classes. The
1262	  whole assembly follows the Mathlib \(\mathtt{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\cdots))\)
1263	  template.
1264	\end{proof}

1266	\section{Consumer: the \(\AddCommGroup\) instance for the relative Picard sheaf}
1267	\label{sec:tensorobj_consumer_acg}
1268	
1269	The substrate of \cref{sec:tensorobj_substrate,sec:tensorobj_onproduct_lift}
1270	supplies all the data missing from \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}'s Lean
1271	encoding. The consumer instance below packages the abelian-group law
1272	\(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]
1273	 := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{C \times_S T} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\) on the
1274	relative Picard quotient via the substrate.
1275	
1276	\begin{theorem}
1277	
1278	\leanok
1279	[Abelian-group instance on the relative Picard quotient via \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)]
1280	  \label{thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj}

1286	        lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,
1287	        lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,

1290	        lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
1291	
1292	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
1293	  % Definitions df:aPf + df:Pfs (the absolute and relative Picard functors are
1294	  % abelian-group-valued; the relative one is defined as a quotient of abelian
1295	  % groups)
1296	  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
1297	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1298	  % "\begin{dfn}\label{df:aPf}
1299	  %  The {\it absolute Picard functor} $\Pic_X$ is the functor from  the
1300	  %  category of (locally Noetherian) $S$-schemes $T$ to the category of
1301	  % abelian groups defined by the formula
1302	  % 	$$\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).$$
1303	  % \end{dfn}
1304	  %  [\dots]
1305	  % \begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
1306	  %   The {\it relative Picard functor} $\Pic_{X/S}$ is defined by
1307	  % 	$$\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).$$
1308	  %  Denote its associated sheaves in the Zariski, \'etale, and fppf
1309	  % topologies  by
1310	  % 	$$\Pic_{(X/S)\zar},\ \Pic_{(X/S)\et},\
1311	  % 	\Pic_{(X/S)\fppf}.$$
1312	  %  \end{dfn}"
1313	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2,
1314	  Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs (the target category is the category of abelian
1315	  groups; the relative Picard functor is a quotient of abelian groups).}
1316	  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve over a
1317	  field \(k\), let \(\pi_C : C \to \Spec k\) be the structure morphism,
1318	  and let \(T\) be a \(k\)-scheme with structure morphism
1319	  \(\pi_T : T \to \Spec k\). The set
1320	  \[
1321	    \Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T) \;\;:=\;\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \,/\, \pi_T^*\Pic(T)
1322	  \]
1323	  of \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined} carries a canonical
1324	  abelian-group structure with addition
1325	  \(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]
1326	   := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{C \times_k T} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\),
1327	  neutral element \(\bigl[\,\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\,\bigr]\), and
1328	  inverse
1329	  \(-\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}^{-1}\,\bigr]\),
1330	  where \(\otimes_{C \times_k T}\) and \((-)^{-1}\) are the operations
1331	  supplied by the substrate of \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
1332	  restricted to the \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) carrier
1333	  via \cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}. With this structure the quotient
1334	  map
1335	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T) \twoheadrightarrow \Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) is a
1336	  surjective group homomorphism with kernel exactly \(\pi_T^*\Pic(T)\),
1337	  in agreement with \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}.
1338	\end{theorem}
1339	
1340	\begin{proof}
1341	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct,
1342	        lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj,
1343	        def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
1344	        lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,
1345	        thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined,
1346	        lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
1347	  Write \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) for the set of isomorphism classes of objects
1348	  of \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) (line bundles on
1349	  \(C \times_k T\)), with the operation
1350	  \([L] + [L'] := [L \otimes_{C \times_k T} L']\). This operation is
1351	  well-defined on isomorphism classes: an isomorphism \(L \cong L_0\) tensors
1352	  to an isomorphism \(L \otimes L' \cong L_0 \otimes L'\) by functoriality of
1353	  \(\otimes\) (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}), and
1354	  symmetrically in the second argument. The abelian-group structure on
1355	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is then exactly the \emph{units group of the
1356	  commutative monoid of \(\otimes\)-iso-classes}
1357	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}): multiplication
1358	  \([L]\cdot[L'] = [L \otimes_{C\times_k T} L']\) is well-defined and
1359	  commutative-monoidal by the coherence isomorphisms
1360	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso},
1361	  and an iso-class is a unit precisely when its carrier is
1362	  \(\otimes\)-invertible (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), the inverse
1363	  being carried by the predicate. Each axiom is a \emph{proposition} --- the
1364	  existence of an isomorphism --- so no monoidal-coherence datum (pentagon,
1365	  triangle, hexagon) is invoked; the \(\AddCommGroup\) on
1366	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is the additive form of this units group. This mirrors
1367	  Mathlib's own Picard group: \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R\) carries its
1368	  \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) (\(\mathtt{instCommGroupPic}\),
1369	  \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}) transported from
1370	  \(\mathrm{Units}\bigl(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R)\bigr)\); here
1371	  the same \(\mathrm{Units}(\cdots)\) shape is transported to the scheme-level
1372	  \(\otimes_X\). (The scheme-level Picard group is genuinely absent from
1373	  Mathlib --- \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup} records ``connect to
1374	  invertible sheaves'' as an open task --- so the project supplies it lightly.)
1375	  The invertibility-predicate carrier is the project's \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\)
1376	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), the scheme-level analogue of
1377	  Mathlib's \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\); the geometric predicate
1378	  \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) is retained separately and is connected to
1379	  \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\) only by an off-critical-path equivalence (only the
1380	  geometric \(\mathtt{IsInvertible} \Leftarrow \mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\)-style
1381	  direction is nontrivial, and it is not needed here).
1382	
1383	  By \cref{lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj}, the pullback functor
1384	  \(\pi_T^{*} : \texttt{LineBundle}\,T \to
1385	   \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) carries tensor products to
1386	  tensor products and the unit to the unit, so the induced map on isomorphism
1387	  classes \(\pi_T^{*} : \Pic(T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T)\) is a group
1388	  homomorphism, and its image is a subgroup.
1389	
1390	  By \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined}, the underlying set of
1391	  \(\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) is the set of cosets of this subgroup. The
1392	  quotient of an abelian group by a subgroup is itself an abelian group, via
1393	  the standard \(\mathtt{QuotientAddGroup}\) construction, and the quotient
1394	  map is a surjective group homomorphism whose kernel is exactly the subgroup
1395	  (Stacks tag 01CR for the Picard-group special case; the general
1396	  quotient-of-abelian-groups statement is in any algebra textbook). Putting
1397	  the pieces together gives the \(\AddCommGroup\) instance on
1398	  \(\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) of the statement, and identifies it with the
1399	  structure of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}.
1400	\end{proof}
1401	
1402	\section{Out of scope}
1403	\label{sec:tensorobj_out_of_scope}
1404	
1405	The following are downstream of this chapter and live in dedicated
1406	sibling chapters; this chapter does \emph{not} touch them:
1407	\begin{itemize}
1408	  \item \textbf{The sheafified relative Picard functor and its abelian-group target}
1409	    (\cref{def:rel_pic_etale_sheafification,
1410	              thm:rel_pic_etale_sheaf_group_structure}) --- once
1411	    \cref{thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj} is established, the
1412	    \(\AddCommGroup\)-valued presheaf, its \'etale sheafification, and
1413	    the sheafification preserving the abelian-group target close
1414	    against it (chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}); none of those
1415	    declarations is the responsibility of this chapter.
1416	  \item \textbf{Representability of \(\Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et}\) by a scheme}
1417	    (A.2.c, \cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}) --- consumes the
1418	    full \(\AddCommGroup\)-valued sheafified presheaf;
1419	    representability is downstream of \cref{thm:rel_pic_etale_sheaf_group_structure}.
1420	  \item \textbf{Identity component \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\)} (A.3,
1421	    \cref{chap:Picard_IdentityComponent}) --- once representability is
1422	    in hand, the identity component is cut out by Hilbert polynomials;
1423	    that is independent of the tensor-product substrate of this chapter.
1424	  \item \textbf{Albanese universal property} (A.4,
1425	    \cref{chap:Albanese_AlbaneseUP}) --- consumes the full Route~A stack
1426	    downstream of A.3.
1427	  \item \textbf{Mathlib upstream PR of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)} ---
1428	    the substrate of this chapter is project-side, intentionally; a
1429	    parallel Mathlib upstream PR is welcome but not on the critical
1430	    path. Once the substrate ships in Mathlib, the
1431	    \texttt{TensorObjSubstrate.lean} file can be slimmed to a single
1432	    import + namespace re-export, but no downstream Lean file changes.
1433	\end{itemize}
1434	
1435	\section{Internal-consistency check}
1436	\label{sec:tensorobj_consistency_check}
1437	
1438	The Lean-pinned declaration blocks of this chapter form a connected
1439	\texttt{\textbackslash uses\{\dots\}} chain whose leaves all resolve
1440	either inside this chapter, inside chapter
1441	\cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback} (A.1.b on disk), or inside
1442	chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor} (A.1.c on disk):
1443	\begin{itemize}
1444	  \item \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} introduces the substrate
1445	    operation and depends on no prior block.
1446	  \item \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality} uses
1447	    \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}.
1448	  \item \cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path} uses
1449	    \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj,
1450	          lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}; it records that the
1451	    full monoidal category is off the critical path, so nothing downstream
1452	    depends on it.
1453	  \item \cref{lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal} uses
1454	    \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}; it is a self-contained
1455	    \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-level supplement, off the critical path and
1456	    \emph{not} an ingredient of \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} --- nothing
1457	    downstream depends on it.
1458	  \item \cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible} (the \(\otimes\)-invertibility
1459	    predicate, carrying the inverse) uses \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}.
1460	  \item The coherence isomorphisms \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
1461	    (left/right unitors) and \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso} (braiding) each use
1462	    only \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}: they are isomorphisms transported
1463	    by \(\mathtt{sheafification.mapIso}\) from the presheaf-level coherence
1464	    data, \emph{not} via \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}. The associator
1465	    \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} uses
1466	    \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj,def:IsLocallyTrivial} together with the
1467	    sheafification-inversion bridge \cref{lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W}: it is
1468	    proved as a three-step composite that absorbs the two nested sheafifications
1469	    by applying \(\mathtt{sheafification}\) to the unit morphism whiskered by a
1470	    locally trivial object (\(M, N, P\) locally trivial), the whiskered unit lying
1471	    in the localizer class \(J.W\) by a local-on-a-cover injectivity argument, with
1472	    the presheaf-level associator transported by \(\mathtt{sheafification.mapIso}\)
1473	    in between. It uses no \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}\) structure, no
1474	    open-immersion restriction-compatibility iso
1475	    (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}), and no sectionwise flatness.
1476	  \item \cref{lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W} (sheafification sends a
1477	    morphism whose underlying map is in \(J.W\) to an isomorphism) depends on no
1478	    prior block; it is the go/no-go bridge consumed by the absorption steps of the
1479	    associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}.
1480	  \item \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer} (left/right whiskering of a
1481	    sheafification-localizer morphism by a flat object stays in the localizer
1482	    class) uses \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}; it is a valid standalone
1483	    result but is \emph{off} the associator critical path (the associator uses the
1484	    local-on-a-cover route, not sectionwise flatness).
1485	  \item \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup} (the commutative monoid of
1486	    \(\otimes\)-iso-classes and its units group) uses
1487	    \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj,
1488	          def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
1489	          lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality,
1490	          lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,
1491	          lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,
1492	          lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}. This is the group-law engine.
1493	  \item The off-critical-path supplements
1494	    \cref{lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal,lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso,%
1495	          lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial,%
1496	          lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible} use
1497	    \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}; none of them is consumed by the group
1498	    law, which rests on \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup} and
1499	    \cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}.
1500	  \item \cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct} restricts the units group to the
1501	    \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\) carrier on \(C \times_S T\), using
1502	    \cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,%
1503	          lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}.
1504	  \item \cref{lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj} uses
1505	    \cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct} and implicitly
1506	    \cref{def:pullback_along_projection} from
1507	    \cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}; it is \emph{not} blocked on
1508	    \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}.
1509	  \item \cref{thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj} uses
1510	    \cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct,
1511	          lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj,
1512	          def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
1513	          lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,
1514	          thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined,
1515	          lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}; the last two are in
1516	    \cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback} (A.1.b) and
1517	    \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor} (A.1.c) respectively.
1518	\end{itemize}

\paragraph{Layer 1: presheaf-of-modules monoidal structure.} Mathlib's
\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.PresheafOfModules} module
constructs a monoidal-category structure on the category
\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\) of presheaves of modules over a presheaf of
commutative rings \(R\); in particular, it exposes a tensor-product
operation \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) realising the
sectionwise tensor product
\[
  (M \otimes_R N)(U) \;\;=\;\; M(U) \otimes_{R(U)} N(U)
\]
for open sets / objects \(U\) in the indexing site, together with the
associator, left/right unitors, and braiding making the resulting
category symmetric monoidal. This is the presheaf-level analogue of
Stacks tag 03DM (relative tensor product of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules).
input.new_string:
\paragraph{Layer 1: presheaf-of-modules monoidal structure (route-(e) source).}
Mathlib's \texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Monoidal} module
constructs a full \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure on the category
\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\) of presheaves of modules over a presheaf of
commutative rings \(R\) --- the instance
\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}\), built on the
\(\mathtt{monoidalCategoryStruct}\) one level down. In particular it exposes a
tensor-product operation \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\)
realising the sectionwise relative tensor product
\[
  (M \otimes_R N)(U) \;\;=\;\; M(U) \otimes_{R(U)} N(U)
\]
for open sets / objects \(U\) in the indexing site, together with the
associator, left/right unitors, braiding, and all coherence (pentagon,
triangle, hexagon) making the resulting category symmetric monoidal. The base
presheaf of rings expected by this instance is
\(R \circ \mathtt{forget}_2\,\mathtt{CommRingCat}\,\mathtt{RingCat}\), which is
definitionally the project's structure-sheaf carrier
\(\mathtt{Sheaf.val}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\); so the presheaf-level monoidal
structure for the \emph{varying} structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X\) is already
available with no project work. This is the presheaf-level analogue of Stacks
tag 03DM (relative tensor product of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules).

\paragraph{The gap.} The substrate \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)
this chapter introduces is the named operation lifting
\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) from Layer 1 into
Layer 2, packaged so that the consumer at chapter
\cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor} can read it as a binary operation on
\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_k T)\). The data this operation must supply for the group law are the monoidal
\emph{coherence isomorphisms} of \(\otimes_X\) --- the associator
(\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}), the left/right unitors
(\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}) and the braiding
(\cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}). The unitors and braiding are obtained by the
cheap pattern \(\mathtt{sheafification.mapIso}\) applied to the corresponding
presheaf-level isomorphism on \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\); the associator,
restricted to the \emph{locally trivial} line bundles the group law actually
multiplies, is obtained as the three-step composite of
\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}: apply the sheafification functor to the unit
morphism whiskered by a locally trivial object to absorb each of the two nested
sheafifications (once on each side), with the presheaf-level associator
transported by \(\mathtt{sheafification.mapIso}\) in between. The only non-formal
input is that the whiskered sheafification unit lies in the localizer class
\(J.W\): local surjectivity is free (right-exactness of \(\otimes\)), and local
injectivity is local-on-target, so it is checked after passing to a trivialising
cover on which the whiskering object is the structure sheaf and the whiskered
unit is carried by the unitor onto the locally injective sheafification unit
itself; the closed bridge \cref{lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W} then inverts it
under sheafification. This uses no flatness hypothesis. Neither route uses the
abstract \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\) adjoint, a
\(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}\) structure on presheaves of modules, or a bundled
\(\mathtt{MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal}\) instance on the localizer class.
Together with the
\(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate \cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}
(which carries the inverse existentially) these assemble the commutative monoid
of \(\otimes\)-iso-classes and its units group
(\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}). The project does \emph{not} require a
global \(\MonoidalCategory\) instance on all quasi-coherent
\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), nor a \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}\) structure:
monoidal coherence beyond these three isomorphisms is never consumed by a group
law on iso-classes (\cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}). The
open-immersion restriction-compatibility isomorphism
\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} is \emph{not} on this critical path and is
retained only as an optional supplement.
The project's policy on this gap is \emph{project-side}: the substrate is
built inside \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/} (the file
\(\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean}\) is its home)
and the consumer instance is closed against it. A parallel Mathlib upstream
PR with the same content is welcome but not on the critical path for the
project; the consumer is closed against the project-side substrate
independently of any upstream timing.
input.new_string:
\paragraph{The gap, and the route-(e) realization.} The substrate
\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\) this chapter introduces is the
named operation lifting \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) from
Layer 1 into Layer 2, packaged so that the consumer at chapter
\cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor} can read it as a binary operation on
\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_k T)\). The end-state monoidal structure on
\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X = \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\)
is obtained from Mathlib's abstract monoidal-localization API rather than by
hand-assembling each coherence isomorphism. It is the instance
\(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\)
(\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.Basic}) applied to
\(\mathcal{C} := \mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,(X.\mathtt{presheaf} \circ
\mathtt{forget}_2\,\mathtt{CommRingCat}\,\mathtt{RingCat})\) (monoidal by
\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}\), Layer 1),
\(L := \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}\) the localization functor, and
\(W := J.W\) the locally-bijective localizer at which sheafification \emph{is}
the localization. Given an instance \([W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}]\) and the unit
comparison \(\varepsilon : L(\mathbf{1}_{\mathcal{C}}) \cong \mathbf{1}\),
\(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) derives the associator, left/right unitors,
braiding, pentagon, triangle, and all naturalities \emph{for free}. The
associator of \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} is then this API-derived associator,
superseding the earlier hand-assembled three-step
\(\mathtt{sheafification.mapIso}\) composite; the unitors and braiding likewise
descend from the API.

The sole genuinely-new project obligation is the instance
\(\mathtt{MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal}\,(J.W)\) for the
\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) sheafification localizer. That class
(\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.Basic}) extends
\(\mathtt{IsMultiplicative}\) and carries two data fields,
\(\mathtt{whiskerLeft} : J.W\,g \Rightarrow J.W\,(F \triangleleft g)\) and
\(\mathtt{whiskerRight} : J.W\,g \Rightarrow J.W\,(g \triangleright F)\), for an
\emph{arbitrary} object \(F\). These are supplied project-side by
\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.W\_whiskerLeft\_of\_W}\) and
\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.W\_whiskerRight\_of\_W}\)
(\cref{lem:whisker_of_W_localizer}), whose load-bearing half is the residual
\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.isLocallyInjective\_whiskerLeft\_of\_W}\)
(\cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whiskerleft_of_W}). The proof technique is
\emph{flatness-free} and is exactly the one Mathlib uses for fixed-base
presheaves in \texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW} (the
instance \([J.\mathtt{HasSheafCompose}\,(\mathtt{forget}\,A)]\),
\([\mathtt{HasEnoughPoints}\,J] \Rightarrow (J.W).\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), proved
stalkwise via \(\mathtt{Functor.Monoidal.map\_tensor}\)): a \(J.W\)-map \(g\) is
a stalkwise iso, so the whiskered map \((F \triangleleft g)_x =
\mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes g_x\) is an iso for \emph{any} \(F\), because tensoring
with an iso preserves isomorphisms --- no flatness and no local triviality.

That Mathlib template is, however, stated for presheaves valued in a
\emph{fixed} monoidal concrete category \(A\) (\(\mathcal{C}^{op} \to A\),
\texttt{Sites.Point.Monoidal} supplying the \(\mathtt{OplaxMonoidal}\) fibre
functor) and does \emph{not} apply to \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\), whose
fibres are modules over a \emph{varying} ring. Mathlib has \emph{no} monoidal
\(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\) and \emph{no} \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) stalk /
fibre / point infrastructure (only \texttt{Presheaf/ColimitFunctor.lean}), so
\((J.W).\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) for the \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) localizer
must be built project-side. The port supplies the two Mathlib-absent ingredients
at the \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) level: (d.1) the stalkwise-iso
characterisation of the module-level \(J.W\) on \(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\), and (d.2)
the commutation of the stalk (a filtered colimit) with the relative
module-presheaf tensor, \((A \otimes^{p} B)_x \cong A_x \otimes_{R_x} B_x\).
Both are isolated as the residual of
\cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whiskerleft_of_W}.

A section-level cover attempt preserving local injectivity \emph{alone} (the
flat-whisker route) is a dead end without flatness: \(\ker(\mathrm{id} \otimes
g)\) is not generated by \(F \otimes \ker g\) (it requires \(\mathrm{Tor}_1\)).
The combined local bijectivity packaged by \(J.W\) sidesteps this precisely by
routing through the stalk iso. The flat variants
\(\mathtt{W\_whiskerLeft/Right\_of\_flat}\) (\cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer})
are valid standalone but off the critical path.

Together with the \(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate
\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible} (which carries the inverse
existentially), the API-derived monoidal structure assembles the commutative
monoid of \(\otimes\)-iso-classes and its units group
(\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}), mirroring Mathlib's
\(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic} = \mathrm{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}\,(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R))\).
No \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}\) structure appears anywhere in this hypothesis
chain. The open-immersion restriction-compatibility isomorphism
\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} is \emph{not} on this critical path and is
retained only as an optional supplement.
The project's policy on this gap is \emph{project-side}: the substrate and the
\((J.W).\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) instance are built inside
\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/} (the file
\(\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean}\) is their home) and
the consumer instance is closed against them. A parallel Mathlib upstream PR
adding monoidal \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\) is welcome but not on the critical
path; the consumer is closed against the project-side substrate independently of
any upstream timing.

input.old_string:
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, def:IsLocallyTrivial,
        lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W}
  The associator is built directly from the presheaf-of-modules monoidal data by
  absorbing the two nested sheafifications. The only non-formal input is that the
  whiskered sheafification unit lies in the sheafification localizer class
  \(J.W\); this is proved at the presheaf/section level on a trivialising cover,
  using local triviality of the whiskering object and no flatness hypothesis.

  \emph{Notation.} Write \(a = \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}\) for the
  sheafification functor and \(M^{\flat}\) for the underlying presheaf of an
  object \(M\), so that the substrate of \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} is
  \(M \otimes_X N = a\bigl(M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}\bigr)\), with
  \(\otimes^{p}\) the presheaf-of-modules tensor product
  (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.PresheafOfModules}). Let
  \(\eta = \mathtt{toSheafify}\) be the sheafification unit, the canonical
  morphism \(A \to a(A)^{\flat}\); it lies in the sheafification localizer class
  \(J.W\), being locally injective (\(\mathtt{isLocallyInjective\_toSheafify}\))
  and locally surjective. Let \(\alpha\) be the associator of the
  presheaf-of-modules monoidal category,
  \[
    \alpha \;\colon\;
      (M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}) \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}
        \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
      M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} (N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}).
  \]

  \emph{The key claim: a whiskered unit stays in \(J.W\).} We show that for a
  \emph{locally trivial} object \(F\) and any presheaf \(A\) the right-whiskered
  unit \(\eta_A \triangleright F^{\flat}\) lies in \(J.W\) (and symmetrically the
  left-whiskered unit \(F^{\flat} \triangleleft \eta_A\)). By the locally-bijective
  characterisation of \(J.W\) it suffices to check local surjectivity and local
  injectivity.

  \emph{Local surjectivity is free.} The presheaf tensor is right exact, so
  whiskering carries the (locally zero) cokernel of \(\eta_A\) to the cokernel of
  \(\eta_A \triangleright F^{\flat}\); hence the whiskered map is locally
  surjective (\(\mathtt{isLocallySurjective\_whiskerLeft}\)), with no hypothesis on
  \(F\).

  \emph{Local injectivity is local on the target, and is supplied by the
  trivialising cover.} Local injectivity asks that the equalizer sieve of the
  morphism be a covering sieve; this is a \emph{local-on-target} condition, so it
  may be checked after passing to a cover. Since \(F\) is locally trivial
  (\cref{def:IsLocallyTrivial}), there is an open cover \(\{V \hookrightarrow U\}\)
  of each open \(U\) on which \(F\) restricts to the structure sheaf,
  \(F^{\flat}|_V \cong \mathcal{O}_X|_V\) --- a sectionwise consequence of the
  trivialisation \(F|_V \cong \mathcal{O}_X|_V\) furnished by
  \(\mathtt{restrictIsoUnitOfLE}\). The presheaf tensor product is
  sectionwise/definitional,
  \[
    (A \otimes^{p} F^{\flat}).\mathrm{obj}\,V
      \;=\; A.\mathrm{obj}\,V \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(V)} F^{\flat}(V),
  \]
  so over \(V\) the trivialisation \(F^{\flat}(V) \cong \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) and the
  right unitor \(\rho\) of the presheaf monoidal structure carry
  \((\eta_A \triangleright F^{\flat})|_V\) onto \(\eta_A|_V\) itself. Now
  \(\eta_A = \mathtt{toSheafify}\) is locally injective
  (\(\mathtt{isLocallyInjective\_toSheafify}\)), hence so is its restriction to the
  cover; gluing the per-\(V\) injectivity along the cover, \(\eta_A \triangleright
  F^{\flat}\) is locally injective. (The sieve bookkeeping is exactly that of the
  flat-whiskering injectivity argument
  \(\mathtt{isLocallyInjective\_whiskerLeft\_of\_flat}\); its
  \(\mathtt{Module.Flat.lTensor\_exact}\) injectivity step is the only thing
  swapped --- replaced here by the trivialisation
  \(F^{\flat}(V) \cong \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) on the cover and the right unitor.) The
  left-whiskered statement \(F^{\flat} \triangleleft \eta_A\) is identical with the
  left unitor \(\lambda\) in place of \(\rho\).

  Local injectivity together with local surjectivity gives
  \(\eta_A \triangleright F^{\flat} \in J.W\) (resp.\ \(F^{\flat} \triangleleft
  \eta_A \in J.W\)). Applying the closed bridge
  \cref{lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W}, the sheafification \(a\) sends each such
  whiskered unit to an isomorphism.

  \emph{The nested form.} Unfolding the substrate on both sides exposes an inner
  sheafification:
  \[
    (M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P
      \;=\; a\Bigl(\, a\bigl(M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}\bigr)^{\flat}
              \otimes^{p} P^{\flat} \,\Bigr),
    \qquad
    M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)
      \;=\; a\Bigl(\, M^{\flat} \otimes^{p}
              a\bigl(N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}\bigr)^{\flat} \,\Bigr).
  \]
  The required isomorphism is the three-step composite:
  \begin{enumerate}
    \item \emph{Absorb the inner sheafification on the left.} The unit
      \(\eta_{M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}}\) lies in \(J.W\); its
      right-whiskering by the locally trivial object \(P\),
      \(\eta_{M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}} \triangleright P^{\flat}\), again
      lies in \(J.W\) by the key claim above (\(P\) locally trivial). By
      \cref{lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W} the sheafification functor \(a\)
      inverts it:
      \[
        a\Bigl(\, a\bigl(M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}\bigr)^{\flat}
              \otimes^{p} P^{\flat} \,\Bigr)
          \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
        a\bigl(\, (M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}) \otimes^{p} P^{\flat} \,\bigr).
      \]
    \item \emph{Transport the presheaf associator.} Apply \(a\) to \(\alpha\):
      \[
        a\bigl(\, (M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}) \otimes^{p} P^{\flat} \,\bigr)
          \;\xrightarrow[\ \sim\ ]{\;a.\mathtt{mapIso}\,\alpha\;}\;
        a\bigl(\, M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} (N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}) \,\bigr).
      \]
    \item \emph{Restore the inner sheafification on the right.} The unit
      \(\eta_{N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}}\) lies in \(J.W\); its
      left-whiskering by the locally trivial object \(M\),
      \(M^{\flat} \triangleleft \eta_{N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}}\), again
      lies in \(J.W\) by the key claim above (\(M\) locally trivial). By
      \cref{lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W} \(a\) inverts it:
      \[
        a\bigl(\, M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} (N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}) \,\bigr)
          \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
        a\Bigl(\, M^{\flat} \otimes^{p}
              a\bigl(N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}\bigr)^{\flat} \,\Bigr)
          \;=\; M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P).
      \]
  \end{enumerate}
  Composing the three isomorphisms yields
  \((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \xrightarrow{\sim} M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\).
  Only steps 1 and 3 carry non-formal content, namely the key claim that the
  whiskered unit lies in \(J.W\); step 2 is the image under \(a\) of the presheaf
  monoidal associator already supplied by Mathlib.

  This is an objectwise isomorphism for the three fixed locally trivial objects,
  not a natural associator on a category: no pentagon identity is constructed or
  used. The group law on \(\otimes\)-iso-classes
  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}) consumes only the existence of this
  isomorphism, \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\,\cdots \cong \cdots)\), so this is all that is
  required. The construction works \emph{entirely at the presheaf/section level on
  a trivialising cover}: each absorption isomorphism comes from checking the
  local-on-target injectivity of a whiskered sheafification unit after restricting
  to a cover on which the whiskering object is the structure sheaf, then inverting
  under \(a\) via \cref{lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W}. It uses
  \emph{no} \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R)\) and
  \emph{no} open-immersion restriction-compatibility isomorphism
  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}): because the presheaf tensor is sectionwise,
  restriction to the cover is plain evaluation at smaller opens, and no
  sheafified-tensor pullback-compatibility iso is needed.
\end{proof}
input.new_string:
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:jw_ismonoidal_localized}
  The associator is the one Mathlib's monoidal-localization API derives
  automatically. By \cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal_localized}, the sheafification
  localizer carries an instance \((J.W).\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), so
  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\) equips the
  localized category --- which is
  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X = \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\),
  the localization of the (monoidal, by
  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}\)) presheaf category at \(J.W\)
  along \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}\) --- with a full
  \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure. The substrate \(\otimes_X\) of
  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} is the tensor of this monoidal structure (up
  to the definitional identification of the sheafified presheaf tensor with the
  localized tensor), and the associator
  \[
    (M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P
      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
    M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)
  \]
  is the component at \((M, N, P)\) of the API-derived associator. No coherence
  identity has to be discharged by hand: pentagon, triangle, unitor and braiding
  naturalities are all part of the \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) output.

  Because the API associator exists for \emph{arbitrary} objects of
  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), the \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) hypotheses on
  \(M, N, P\) in the statement are vestigial: they are received only to match the
  current \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_assoc\_iso}\) signature and play no role in the
  construction. They may be dropped once the API-derived associator replaces the
  pinned signature. As before, the group law on \(\otimes\)-iso-classes
  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}) consumes only the existence of this
  isomorphism, \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\,\cdots \cong \cdots)\); no pentagon identity
  is consumed by a proposition-valued group law. The construction uses no
  \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R)\) structure and no
  open-immersion restriction-compatibility isomorphism
  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}); its sole non-formal input is the
  \((J.W).\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) instance of \cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal_localized},
  whose load-bearing residual is
  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whiskerleft_of_W}.
\end{proof}

\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
[Whiskering a locally-bijective map preserves local injectivity (residual)]
  \label{lem:islocallyinjective_whiskerleft_of_W}
  \lean{PresheafOfModules.isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W}
  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
  Let \(R\) be a presheaf of commutative rings on a site with topology \(J\), and
  let \(J.W\) be the sheafification localizer (the locally bijective morphisms).
  For an \emph{arbitrary} presheaf of \(R\)-modules \(F\) and a morphism \(g\) of
  presheaves of \(R\)-modules whose underlying map lies in \(J.W\), the
  left-whiskered morphism \(F \triangleleft g = \mathrm{id}_F \otimes^{p} g\) is
  \(J\)-locally injective.

  This is the load-bearing half of the \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\) field of
  \(\mathtt{MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal}\,(J.W)\)
  (\cref{lem:whisker_of_W_localizer}), and is \textbf{the sole remaining open
  obligation for the entire \(\otimes\)-group-law engine}. It is proved
  \emph{stalkwise}: a \(J.W\)-map \(g\) is a stalk isomorphism, so
  \((F \triangleleft g)_x = \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes g_x\) is an isomorphism at
  every point \(x\) (tensoring with an isomorphism preserves isomorphisms --- no
  flatness, no local triviality), whence \(F \triangleleft g\) is locally
  injective. Carrying this argument out at the \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) level
  requires two ingredients absent from Mathlib at the pinned commit, which
  constitute the residual: (d.1) the stalkwise-iso characterisation of the
  module-level \(J.W\) on \(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\), and (d.2) the commutation of the
  stalk (a filtered colimit) with the relative module-presheaf tensor product,
  \((A \otimes^{p} B)_x \cong A_x \otimes_{R_x} B_x\). The same argument is run by
  Mathlib for presheaves valued in a fixed monoidal concrete category in
  \texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW}; that file does not
  apply to \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\) (varying base ring), so the port is
  project-side.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}
[Whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer (flatness-free)]
  \label{lem:whisker_of_W_localizer}
  \lean{PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerLeft_of_W, PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_W}
  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:islocallyinjective_whiskerleft_of_W}
  With \(R\), \(J\) and \(J.W\) as in
  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whiskerleft_of_W}, for an arbitrary presheaf of
  \(R\)-modules \(F\) and any \(g \in J.W\) the whiskered morphisms
  \(F \triangleleft g\) and \(g \triangleright F\) again lie in \(J.W\). No
  flatness or local-triviality hypothesis on \(F\) is required.

  Membership in \(J.W\) is local bijectivity, i.e.\ local surjectivity together
  with local injectivity. Local surjectivity is free for any \(F\) by
  right-exactness of the presheaf tensor
  (\(\mathtt{isLocallySurjective\_whiskerLeft}\)). Local injectivity is exactly
  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whiskerleft_of_W}. The right-whiskered statement
  \(g \triangleright F\) follows by conjugating with the braiding of the symmetric
  presheaf-of-modules monoidal structure, an isomorphism that preserves \(J.W\).
  These two facts are precisely the \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\) and
  \(\mathtt{whiskerRight}\) data of the instance
  \(\mathtt{MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal}\,(J.W)\)
  (\cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal_localized}).
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}
[Route (e): \((J.W).\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) and the localized monoidal category]
  \label{lem:jw_ismonoidal_localized}
  \uses{lem:whisker_of_W_localizer}
  The whisker stability of \cref{lem:whisker_of_W_localizer} furnishes the two
  data fields of \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal}\,(J.W)\)
  (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.Basic}), whose remaining
  field --- multiplicativity (\(\mathtt{IsMultiplicative}\)) --- holds for any
  sheafification localizer. Hence \(J.W\) is a monoidal morphism property on the
  monoidal category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,(X.\mathtt{presheaf} \circ
  \mathtt{forget}_2\,\mathtt{CommRingCat}\,\mathtt{RingCat})\)
  (\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}\)).

  Feeding this instance, the localization functor
  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}\), and the unit comparison
  \(\varepsilon : L(\mathbf{1}) \cong \mathbf{1}\) to
  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\) endows the
  localized category --- definitionally
  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X = \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\)
  --- with a \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure whose tensor is \(\otimes_X\) and
  whose associator, unitors, braiding and all coherence are derived by the API.
  This is the route-(e) target. The construction is genuinely Mathlib-absent at
  the pinned commit (there is no monoidal \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\) and no
  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) stalk/point infrastructure), so it is supplied
  project-side; the only non-formal residual it rests on is
  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whiskerleft_of_W}.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[Associator for $\otimes_X$ on locally trivial objects]

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[Closure of \(\otimes\) on the \(\otimes\)-invertible carrier
  \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\)]
  \label{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjOnProduct}
  \uses{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
        lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,
        lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
  % NOTE (iter-213): prose↔Lean predicate mismatch. The Lean `tensorObjOnProduct`
  % takes `LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT`, which is the IsLocallyTrivial subtype
  % (defined in LineBundlePullback.lean), and its body uses only
  % `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` directly — NOT `IsInvertible`,
  % `tensorObjIsoclassCommMonoid`, or the coherence isos as the prose/\uses below
  % claim. Blueprint-writer should reconcile the carrier predicate (IsLocallyTrivial
  % vs IsInvertible) and the \uses. See task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-ts213.md.
  % NOTE: the coherence isos
  % \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}
  % and the units-of-monoid engine \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup} supply
  % the group-law data. \texttt{IsLocallyTrivial} is a separate predicate,
  % connected to \texttt{IsInvertible} only by an off-critical-path equivalence.
  Let \(C \to S\) and \(T \to S\) be \(S\)-schemes. The carrier is the
  \(\otimes\)-invertible subtype
  \[
    \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T
    \;\;=\;\;
    \bigl\{\,M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_S T)
            \,\bigm|\, \mathtt{IsInvertible}\,M \,\bigr\}
  \]
  of \cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}. The bifunctor
  \(\otimes_{C \times_S T}\) of
  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality} restricts to this subtype:
  if \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\,M\) and \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\,M'\) then
  \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}(M \otimes_{C \times_S T} M')\); the unit
  \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T}\) is invertible; and every invertible module
  carries its own tensor inverse. Hence the isomorphism classes of the subtype
  form the units group of \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup} on
  \(C \times_S T\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
        lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,
        lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
  Closure under \(\otimes\) is the units-closed-under-multiplication statement of
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}: if \(M \otimes N \cong \mathcal{O}\)
  and \(M' \otimes N' \cong \mathcal{O}\), then
  \((M \otimes M') \otimes (N \otimes N') \cong \mathcal{O}\) by the coherence
  isomorphisms, so \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}(M \otimes M')\) with witness
  \(N \otimes N'\). The unit \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T}\) is invertible with
  witness itself (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}), and the tensor inverse is
  carried by the predicate (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}). Thus the
  subtype is a submonoid all of whose elements are units --- the abelian group
  of iso-classes that is the units group of
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup} restricted to \(C \times_S T\).
\end{proof}
input.new_string:
\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[Closure of \(\otimes\) on the locally-trivial carrier
  \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\)]
  \label{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjOnProduct}
  \uses{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
  Let \(C \to S\) and \(T \to S\) be \(S\)-schemes. The carrier
  \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) (introduced in
  \cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}) is the \emph{locally-trivial} subtype
  \[
    \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T
    \;\;=\;\;
    \bigl\{\,M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_S T)
            \,\bigm|\, \mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\,M \,\bigr\}
  \]
  --- the \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) predicate, \emph{not} the
  \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\) predicate. The substrate tensor product
  \(\otimes_{C \times_S T}\) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} restricts to
  this subtype: if \(M\) and \(M'\) are locally trivial then so is
  \(M \otimes_{C \times_S T} M'\). This closure is the content of the Lean
  \(\mathtt{tensorObjOnProduct}\), whose body invokes
  \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_isLocallyTrivial}\)
  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}) directly. The structure sheaf
  \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T}\) is locally trivial, so \(\otimes_{C \times_S T}\)
  makes the locally-trivial line bundles on \(C \times_S T\) a monoid; the group
  law and its inverse are assembled separately in
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj}.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
  Closure under \(\otimes_{C \times_S T}\) is exactly
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}: the tensor product of two
  locally-trivial rank-one modules is locally trivial of rank one, established on
  a common affine trivialising cover where both factors are free of rank one. The
  Lean \(\mathtt{tensorObjOnProduct}\) packages this closure as the action of
  \(\otimes_{C \times_S T}\) on the \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) subtype; it does
  not invoke \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\), the iso-class monoid, or the coherence
  isomorphisms, which enter only in the downstream group-law assembly.
\end{proof}

1	\chapter{Relative Picard sheaf --- \texttt{Scheme.Modules.tensorObj} substrate (A.1.c.SubT)}
2	\label{chap:Picard_TensorObjSubstrate}
3	
4	\section{Motivation: the abelian-group-valued relative Picard sheaf}
5	\label{sec:tensorobj_motivation}
6	
7	Fix a base field \(k\) and a smooth proper geometrically integral curve \(C\) over
8	\(k\). In Kleiman's framework (\cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor},
9	\cref{def:rel_pic_sharp}), the relative Picard functor
10	\[
11	  \Pic_{C/k} \;\;\colon\;\; (\Sch/k)^{op} \;\longrightarrow\; \AddCommGrpCat,
12	  \qquad
13	  T \;\longmapsto\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \, / \, \pi_T^* \Pic(T),
14	\]
15	is, by definition, abelian-group-valued. The abelian-group structure on each
16	fiber \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) comes from tensor product of line bundles
17	(Stacks tag 01CR / Hartshorne~II.\S 6), and the structure descends to the
18	quotient because \(\pi_T^*\) is a group homomorphism.
19	
20	% SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
21	% Definitions df:aPf + df:Pfs (the absolute and relative Picard functors are
22	% abelian-group-valued; the relative one is defined as a quotient of abelian
23	% groups)
24	% (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
25	% SOURCE QUOTE:
26	% "\begin{dfn}\label{df:aPf}
27	%  The {\it absolute Picard functor} $\Pic_X$ is the functor from  the
28	%  category of (locally Noetherian) $S$-schemes $T$ to the category of
29	% abelian groups defined by the formula
30	% 	$$\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).$$
31	% \end{dfn}
32	%  [\dots]
33	% \begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
34	%   The {\it relative Picard functor} $\Pic_{X/S}$ is defined by
35	% 	$$\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).$$
36	%  Denote its associated sheaves in the Zariski, \'etale, and fppf
37	% topologies  by
38	% 	$$\Pic_{(X/S)\zar},\ \Pic_{(X/S)\et},\
39	% 	\Pic_{(X/S)\fppf}.$$
40	%  \end{dfn}"
41	\textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2, Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs.
42	The absolute and relative Picard functors take values in the category of
43	abelian groups; the relative functor is the quotient of one such group by
44	another, and the structure morphisms of the resulting presheaf are group
45	homomorphisms.}
46	
47	Concretely, the abelian-group law on \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is the assignment
48	\(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr] :=
49	\bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\),
50	with neutral element the class of the structure sheaf
51	\(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\) and inverse
52	\(- \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}^{-1}\,\bigr]\), where
53	\(\mathcal{L}^{-1} = \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}}(\mathcal{L},
54	\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T})\) is the dual invertible sheaf. Pullback of
55	\(\mathcal{O}\)-modules along \(\pi_T : C \times_k T \to T\) preserves tensor
56	products and the structure sheaf, so the image
57	\(\pi_T^* \Pic(T) \subset \Pic(C \times_k T)\) is a subgroup, and the quotient
58	\(\Pic(C \times_k T) / \pi_T^* \Pic(T)\) inherits a canonical abelian-group
59	structure. This is the content of
60	\cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} in chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}.
61	
62	The mathematics is straightforward; the obstacle to formalisation is purely
63	infrastructural, and --- crucially --- much smaller than a full
64	monoidal-category construction. The group law lives on \emph{isomorphism
65	classes} of line bundles: each group axiom is a \emph{proposition} asserting
66	the existence of an isomorphism (\(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\,\cdots \cong \cdots)\)),
67	and a proposition is insensitive to which isomorphism witnesses it. In
68	particular the coherence data of a monoidal category --- the pentagon,
69	triangle and hexagon identities, and a fortiori any closed structure --- is
70	\emph{never consumed} by a group law on iso-classes. What the law genuinely
71	requires on the carrier is therefore only:
72	\begin{enumerate}
73	  \item a binary tensor-product operation
74	    \(\otimes\, : \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \times \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
75	                  \to \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), well-defined on
76	    isomorphism classes;
77	  \item the structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X\) as a designated unit object
78	    for \(\otimes\);
79	  \item the \(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate
80	    (\(\exists N,\ M \otimes_X N \cong \mathcal{O}_X\)) together with the three
81	    monoidal coherence isomorphisms of \(\otimes_X\): associativity
82	    \((M \otimes N) \otimes P \cong M \otimes (N \otimes P)\), unit
83	    \(\mathcal{O}_X \otimes M \cong M\) (and on the right), and commutativity
84	    \(M \otimes N \cong N \otimes M\). The inverse is carried by the
85	    predicate, not by a separate lemma.
86	\end{enumerate}
87	At Mathlib's pinned commit, the entire monoidal scaffolding for (3) is already
88	available abstractly, and the project's task is to \emph{instantiate} it rather
89	than to hand-assemble the coherence data. Two facts drive this. First, the
90	category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\) of presheaves of modules over a
91	presheaf of commutative rings \(R\) is already a (symmetric) monoidal category in
92	Mathlib (\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}\)), with the sectionwise
93	relative tensor \((M \otimes_R N)(U) = M(U) \otimes_{R(U)} N(U)\); this applies
94	verbatim to the \emph{varying} structure sheaf \(R = \mathcal{O}_X\). Second,
95	Mathlib's localization-of-monoidal-categories API
96	(\(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\)) takes a
97	monoidal category \(C\), a localization functor \(L : C \to D\) at a morphism
98	property \(W\), and an instance \(W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), and produces a
99	\emph{full} monoidal-category structure on the localization \(D\) --- associator,
100	left/right unitors, braiding, and \emph{all} coherence (pentagon, triangle,
101	naturalities) derived. Sheafification of presheaves of modules is exactly the
102	localization \(L = \mathtt{sheafification}\) at the locally-bijective class
103	\(W = J.W\), so the monoidal structure on \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) is
104	\emph{free} from this API once \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) is supplied.
105	
106	Consequently the only genuinely-new obligation is the single instance
107	\(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) for the sheafification localizer on
108	\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\). Its two fields are the
109	left/right whiskering-stability statements \(J.W\,g \Rightarrow J.W(F \triangleleft
110	g)\) and \(J.W\,g \Rightarrow J.W(g \triangleright F)\) for an \emph{arbitrary}
111	module \(F\) (\cref{lem:whisker_of_W}), and these are flatness-free: a
112	\(J.W\)-morphism is a stalkwise isomorphism, so \((F \triangleleft g)_x =
113	\mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes g_x\) is again an isomorphism (tensoring with an
114	isomorphism preserves isomorphisms --- no flatness, no local triviality). The
115	load-bearing half is the local-injectivity statement
116	\cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}, whose stalkwise proof needs two
117	\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\)-level ingredients that are Mathlib-absent at the
118	pinned commit (the module-level stalk characterisation of \(J.W\), and the
119	commutation of the stalk with the relative tensor); this is the
120	\emph{sole remaining open obligation} for the whole group-law engine.
121	
122	With \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) in hand, the substrate \(\otimes_X\) carries the
123	associator, unitors and braiding from \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\), and the
124	group law assembles the commutative monoid of \(\otimes\)-iso-classes whose units
125	group is the Picard group (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}), exactly as
126	Mathlib builds \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\) as
127	\(\mathrm{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R))\). The
128	\(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible},
129	Mathlib's \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\) idiom) carries each inverse
130	existentially. The geometric compatibility isomorphism
131	\(\mathtt{tensorObj\_restrict\_iso}\) (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}) and any
132	separate tensor-inverse lemma are \emph{off} this critical path; see also
133	\cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}.
134	
135	\section{Mathlib API survey}
136	\label{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}
137	
138	The relevant pieces of the Mathlib API at the pinned commit
139	(\texttt{b80f227}) assemble into a single \emph{realization route} (route~(e)):
140	the monoidal structure on the substrate is obtained by instantiating Mathlib's
141	abstract localization-of-monoidal-categories API, not by hand-assembling the
142	associator. Four Mathlib facts are the formalization vehicle, all verified
143	against the on-disk Mathlib source.
144	
145	\paragraph{(i) The presheaf-of-modules monoidal category.} Mathlib's
146	\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Monoidal} constructs a
147	monoidal-category instance
148	\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}\) on
149	\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,(R \circ \mathtt{forget}_2\,\mathtt{CommRingCat}\,
150	\mathtt{RingCat})\), with the sectionwise relative tensor
151	\[
152	  (M \otimes_R N)(U) \;\;=\;\; M(U) \otimes_{R(U)} N(U)
153	\]
154	% SOURCE: [Mathlib], `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct` /
155	% `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`, in
156	% `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Monoidal.lean` (L104, L125)
157	% (read from .lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Monoidal.lean).
158	% SOURCE QUOTE:
159	% "noncomputable instance monoidalCategoryStruct :
160	%     MonoidalCategoryStruct (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) where
161	%   tensorObj := tensorObj
162	%   whiskerLeft _ _ _ g := tensorHom (𝟙 _) g
163	%   whiskerRight f _ := tensorHom f (𝟙 _)
164	%   ...
165	%   tensorUnit := unit _
166	%   associator M₁ M₂ M₃ := isoMk (fun _ ↦ α_ _ _ _) ...
167	% noncomputable instance monoidalCategory :
168	%     MonoidalCategory (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) where ..."
169	together with associator, left/right unitors and braiding making it symmetric
170	monoidal. The base presheaf of rings \(R \circ \mathtt{forget}_2\,
171	\mathtt{CommRingCat}\,\mathtt{RingCat}\) is exactly the project's
172	\(\mathtt{Sheaf.val}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\) carrier (equal by \(\mathtt{rfl}\)),
173	so this monoidal structure applies verbatim to the \emph{varying} structure sheaf
174	\(\mathcal{O}_X\) --- no project work. This is the presheaf-level analogue of
175	Stacks tag 03DM (relative tensor product of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules).
176	
177	\paragraph{(ii) The localization-of-monoidal-categories API.} Mathlib's
178	\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.Basic} provides the class
179	\(\mathtt{MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal}\) and the construction
180	\(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\). The class records exactly
181	the whiskering-stability data:
182	% SOURCE: [Mathlib], `CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal` and
183	% `CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal`, in
184	% `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Localization/Monoidal/Basic.lean` (L44, L86, L440)
185	% (read from .lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Localization/Monoidal/Basic.lean).
186	% SOURCE QUOTE:
187	% "class IsMonoidal : Prop extends W.IsMultiplicative where
188	%   whiskerLeft (X : C) {Y₁ Y₂ : C} (g : Y₁ ⟶ Y₂) (hg : W g) : W (X ◁ g)
189	%   whiskerRight {X₁ X₂ : C} (f : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (hf : W f) (Y : C) : W (f ▷ Y)
190	%  ...
191	% def LocalizedMonoidal (L : C ⥤ D) (W : MorphismProperty C)
192	%     [W.IsMonoidal] [L.IsLocalization W] {unit : D} (_ : L.obj (𝟙_ C) ≅ unit) := D
193	%  ...
194	% noncomputable instance :
195	%     MonoidalCategory (LocalizedMonoidal L W ε) where ..."
196	Given \([\MonoidalCategory\,C]\), \([W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}]\), a localization
197	functor \(L : C \to D\) with \([L.\mathtt{IsLocalization}\,W]\) and an isomorphism
198	\(\varepsilon : L(\mathbf{1}_C) \cong \mathtt{unit}\), the localized category
199	\(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\,L\,W\,\varepsilon\) is a \(\MonoidalCategory\) with
200	\emph{all} coherence (associator, unitors, pentagon, triangle, naturalities)
201	derived. There is \emph{no} \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}\) anywhere in the
202	hypothesis chain.
203	
204	\paragraph{(iii) The proof template for $W$-monoidality.} Mathlib's
205	\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW} proves
206	\((J.W\,(A := A)).\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) \emph{stalkwise} for presheaves valued in a
207	\emph{fixed} monoidal concrete category \(A\) (i.e.\ \(C^{op} \to A\)), via the
208	conservative-family characterisation \(\mathtt{hP.W\_iff}\),
209	\(\mathtt{Functor.Monoidal.map\_tensor}\) and \(\mathtt{infer\_instance}\):
210	% SOURCE: [Mathlib], `CategoryTheory.ObjectProperty.IsConservativeFamilyOfPoints.isMonoidal_W`
211	% and the `(J.W).IsMonoidal` instance, in
212	% `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Point/IsMonoidalW.lean` (L48, L57)
213	% (read from .lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Point/IsMonoidalW.lean).
214	% SOURCE QUOTE:
215	% "lemma ObjectProperty.IsConservativeFamilyOfPoints.isMonoidal_W
216	%     [J.HasSheafCompose (forget A)] :
217	%     (J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal :=
218	%   .mk' _ (fun f g hf hg ↦ by
219	%     simp only [hP.W_iff (A := A)] at hf hg ⊢
220	%     intro Φ
221	%     rw [Functor.Monoidal.map_tensor]
222	%     infer_instance)"
223	This is the \emph{template} for the proof technique used in route~(e), but it does
224	\emph{not} apply directly to \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\): modules over a
225	\emph{varying} ring are not of the form \(C^{op} \to A\) for a fixed monoidal
226	\(A\).
227	
228	\paragraph{The gap and route~(e).} The substrate
229	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\) of this chapter is the
230	sheafification of \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\), and
231	sheafification of presheaves of modules is exactly the localization
232	\(L = \mathtt{sheafification}\) at the locally-bijective class \(W = J.W\). Hence
233	the monoidal structure on \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X =
234	\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\) is obtained by applying
235	\(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) (ii) to the already-monoidal category (i) ---
236	\emph{once} the instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) is supplied. That instance is
237	the \emph{sole genuinely-new obligation}: its two whiskering-stability fields are
238	\cref{lem:whisker_of_W}, whose load-bearing local-injectivity half is
239	\cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}, the only remaining open sorry. The
240	proof technique is that of template (iii) ported to \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\):
241	a \(J.W\)-morphism \(g\) is a stalkwise isomorphism, so
242	\((F \triangleleft g)_x = \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes g_x\) is again an isomorphism
243	for \emph{any} \(F\) --- no flatness, no local triviality.
244	
245	What is \emph{genuinely Mathlib-absent} at the pinned commit: there is no
246	monoidal \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\), and no \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\)
247	stalk/fiber/point infrastructure (only \(\mathtt{Presheaf/ColimitFunctor}\)). So
248	\(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) for the \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) sheafification
249	localizer must be built project-side, and the two stalk-level ingredients its
250	proof needs --- (d.1) the module-level stalkwise characterisation of \(J.W\) on
251	\(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\), and (d.2) the commutation \((A \otimes^{p} B)_x \cong A_x
252	\otimes_{R_x} B_x\) of the stalk with the relative module-presheaf tensor --- are
253	the residual ported by \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}. Once
254	\(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) lands, the associator, unitors and braiding are
255	\emph{free} from (ii) and the bespoke three-step composite is superseded.
256	
257	The project's policy on this gap is \emph{project-side}: the substrate is
258	built inside \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/} (the file
259	\(\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean}\) is its home)
260	and the consumer instance is closed against it. A parallel Mathlib upstream

260	and the consumer instance is closed against it. A parallel Mathlib upstream
261	PR with the same content is welcome but not on the critical path for the
